workout.title: Chest Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Chest Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby.
Sit on the bench with the dumbbells resting on your thighs, feet flat on the floor for stability.
Use your legs to help lift the dumbbells as you lie back so the weights are beside your upper chest; hold them with a neutral or slightly supinated grip.
Retract your shoulder blades and press your upper back into the bench so your chest is proud (maintain a stable supine position on the incline).
Plant your feet and brace your core to avoid excessive arching of the spine.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together in a controlled path until your arms are almost fully extended (do not lock the elbows).
Exhale as you press up, pause briefly at the top with control, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start position at the upper chest level.
Keep the elbows at a moderate angle (not flared excessively) and the wrists neutral throughout the movement.
Repeat for desired repetitions and use a spotter for heavy sets or if you cannot safely return the weights to your thighs/stand them up.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline bench press is performed lying supine on an incline bench and pressing dumbbells upward to emphasize the upper portion of the chest. It builds strength and mass in the upper pectorals while also recruiting the front delts and triceps.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Chest Press
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach single hand grips; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach single hand grips; select an appropriate weight.

Stand in the middle of the station facing away from the stacks with one foot slightly forward (staggered stance) for stability, arms bent and handles held at chest level, elbows roughly at chest height.
Brace your core (engage abs), keep a neutral spine and shoulders slightly retracted to set the starting position; wrists remain neutral.
Step forward or press your feet into the floor to create tension in the cables so the handles cannot move without effort.
Exhale and press both handles forward in a controlled motion, extending the arms until nearly straight but without locking the elbows; keep the movement horizontal and drive from the chest.
At full extension, pause briefly and squeeze the pectorals (and serratus anterior) while keeping the torso stable and not overextending the lower back.
Inhale and slowly return the handles to the start position with control, allowing the elbows to bend to chest level while maintaining core tension.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping consistent tempo and avoiding excessive leaning or rotating of the torso.
exercises.description: A standing cable press performed from a dual‑pulley station that emphasizes horizontal pushing to work the chest. The movement also requires shoulder and triceps involvement while the core stabilizes the torso.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and extend the arms straight above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keep a slight, fixed bend in the elbows throughout the movement (do not lock the elbows).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, maintaining the slight elbow bend until you feel a stretch in the chest or your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep the shoulder blades stable on the bench (no excessive shrugging) and avoid letting the dumbbells travel behind the plane of your torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom under control, then exhale and bring the dumbbells back up along the same arc, focusing on squeezing the pectorals until the weights meet above the sternum.
Perform each rep with controlled tempo and avoid bouncing or using momentum; select a weight that allows full control and a safe range of motion.
exercises.description: A supine resistance exercise performed with dumbbells that isolates the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from above the sternum to the sides, producing a deep stretch and concentric squeeze of the pectorals.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single-handle attachment on a cable machine to a high pulley (above shoulder height).
instructions: Set a single-handle attachment on a cable machine to a high pulley (above shoulder height).
Stand facing across from the pulley in a staggered stance (one foot forward for balance) with a slight forward lean at the hips; engage your core and keep a neutral spine.
Reach up and grab the handle with the working hand using a neutral grip; allow the arm to start out to the side with a small bend in the elbow.
Depress and stabilize your shoulder (avoid shrugging) and pull the handle down and across your body in a smooth arc toward the opposite mid-chest, rotating the torso slightly as needed.
At the end of the movement, actively squeeze the pectorals and serratus anterior while maintaining the elbow bend; pause briefly at peak contraction.
Slowly and under control, return the handle to the starting position allowing the chest to stretch but keeping tension on the cable; do not let the weight stack slam.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides. Keep breathing steadily (exhale as you pull across, inhale as you return).
Maintain a slight elbow bend throughout, avoid shoulder hyperextension, and do not use excessive torso rotation or momentum to drive the movement.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable crossover isolates one side of the chest by pulling a cable across the body from a high pulley, emphasizing the pectoral contraction and allowing a strong end-range squeeze. It is performed standing with a slight forward lean and a controlled return to the start position.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Fly
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench between two cable towers (about 30–45° incline).
instructions: Set an incline bench between two cable towers (about 30–45° incline). Attach single D‑handles and set the pulleys to low or just below chest height so the cables travel upward across your chest.
Sit on the bench and lie back so your head rests on the pad; grip one handle in each hand with a neutral or palms‑facing posture and feet flat on the floor.
Retract your shoulder blades and brace your core so your back stays pressed into the bench (inclined supine position). Keep a slight, fixed bend in your elbows throughout the exercise.
Start with your arms out to the sides in line with your chest (open position) and inhale, then exhale as you bring the handles together in a wide arc toward the centerline of your upper chest.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction, focusing on squeezing the pectorals and keeping the elbows slightly bent and stable (avoid driving motion from the wrists or excessive shoulder elevation).
Slowly reverse the movement on the inhale, controlling the cables as you return to the start position until you feel a stretch in the chest without losing scapular retraction.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining core engagement and avoiding excessive momentum or arching of the lower back.
exercises.description: Lying on an incline bench placed between two cable towers, you perform a bilateral fly motion with a slight elbow bend to concentrically bring the handles together and target the upper chest. The movement emphasizes a horizontal adduction arc while the core stabilizes the torso.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

instructions: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.
Lie supine on the incline bench with your head supported, feet flat on the floor, and back slightly arched while maintaining scapular retraction.
Grip the barbell with a pronated grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over your upper chest/shoulder line, then stabilize before starting the descent.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar in a controlled path to the upper chest/clavicular area, keeping elbows at roughly a 30–60° angle from the torso (avoid flaring to 90°).
Lightly touch or stop just above the chest, pause briefly without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward and slightly back to the start position, fully extending the elbows.
Keep core braced and feet planted throughout to maintain stability and prevent excessive lower back extension.
Carefully re-rack the bar under control or have a spotter assist when finished.
exercises.description: A barbell press performed on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest by pressing the bar upward and slightly toward the clavicle. It also engages the front delts and triceps for pressing and locking out the weight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
instructions: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
Step 2: Brace your core and gently retract and depress your shoulder blades to stabilize the shoulder girdle throughout the movement.

Step 3: Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, keeping the same slight bend in your elbows and stopping when your upper arms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the chest (avoid dropping below bench level).

Step 4: Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulders, then exhale and drive the dumbbells back up in the same arc by contracting the pectorals until the weights meet or are close together over your sternum.
Step 5: Maintain controlled tempo (no bouncing), keep elbows fixed at the same angle, and avoid locking the arms at the top; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Lying flat on a bench while holding dumbbells, the dumbbell fly opens the arms in a wide arc and brings them together to isolate and stretch the chest (pectorals). Movement emphasizes a controlled, arcing motion with a slight bend in the elbows to protect the shoulder joint.

exercises.name: Cable Standing-Up Straight Crossovers
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to a high position (around or slightly above shoulder height) and attach single-hand handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to a high position (around or slightly above shoulder height) and attach single-hand handles.
Stand in the center of the cable station with feet hip-width apart (or a slight staggered stance for balance), torso upright and core braced.
Grasp a handle in each hand with a neutral grip, arms out to the sides at about shoulder level and a slight bend in the elbows; retract the shoulder blades slightly to start.

Exhale and pull both handles down and together in a smooth arc across the body until the hands meet or nearly meet in front of the sternum, keeping the elbows slightly bent and avoiding excessive torso lean or rotation.
Pause briefly and squeeze the pectorals at the end range, then inhale and slowly resist the cables as you return your arms to the starting position under control.
Maintain an upright posture, keep the core engaged, avoid shrugging the shoulders, and use a full controlled range of motion rather than momentum.
exercises.description: Standing cable crossovers are a bilateral cable fly variation performed upright that brings the hands together in front of the body to load and concentrically shorten the chest (pectorals). The movement uses a fixed slight elbow bend and controlled return to emphasize chest contraction while the core stabilizes the torso.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Fly
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulleys to a high or slightly above-shoulder position and attach single-handle grips.
instructions: Set the cable pulleys to a high or slightly above-shoulder position and attach single-handle grips.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered (split) stance or feet shoulder-width apart with a slight forward lean from the hips so you have stable balance; brace your core.
Grasp a handle in each hand with a neutral or palms-down grip and take one step forward until you feel light tension in the cables; keep a small bend in the elbows throughout.
Start with your arms out to the sides roughly at shoulder height (or slightly higher for a high-cable fly) and chest lifted; retract your shoulder blades slightly to protect the joints.
Exhale and bring the handles together in a controlled arc in front of your chest at midline, leading with the elbows and squeezing the pectorals at the finish.
Pause briefly with hands meeting or slightly overlapping, then inhale and slowly resist the cables as you return your arms to the start position using the same arcing path.
Keep the movement smooth and avoid locking the elbows, excessive torso rotation, or shrugging the shoulders; maintain core tension for stability.
exercises.description: Standing cable fly performed with the pulleys set high to mid-high engages the chest by bringing the arms together in a wide arc; the shoulders and core stabilize the movement. It emphasizes the pectoral fibers with controlled concentric adduction and slow eccentric return.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Reverse Grip Pulldown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar and grasp it with a reverse (underhand, palms facing you) grip about shoulder-width or slightly narrower.
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown bench with feet flat, knees secured under the thigh pads and torso upright (seated position).
Attach a straight bar and grasp it with a reverse (underhand, palms facing you) grip about shoulder-width or slightly narrower.
Start with arms fully extended overhead, shoulders relaxed and a slight natural arch in the lower back.
Exhale and pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back, bringing the bar to the upper chest while keeping the scapulae down and squeezing the lats.
Pause briefly at the bottom to feel the contraction in the lats and biceps; avoid excessive backward lean.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the start position with controlled movement until arms are fully extended.
Keep wrists neutral and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso; reduce weight if hips or torso begin to move.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a reverse (underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while placing extra demand on the biceps and forearms. Pull the bar down to the upper chest with elbows driving down and back while keeping the torso stable.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Wide grip Row
exercises.notes: Attach a wide, straight or slightly curved bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a pronated (palms down) wide grip, arms extended and torso upright.
instructions: Sit on the bench/seat facing the cable machine with feet braced flat on the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach a wide, straight or slightly curved bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a pronated (palms down) wide grip, arms extended and torso upright.
Set your chest up and maintain a neutral spine with a slight forward lean from the hips — avoid excessive torso rocking or leaning back.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back and out to the sides, squeezing your shoulder blades together and keeping the wrists neutral.
Pause for 1 second at the peak contraction with elbows pulled past the torso and the upper back engaged.
Slowly extend the arms to return the handle to the start under control until you feel a gentle stretch in the upper back; do not let the weight slam forward.
Breathe out during the pull (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders, using excessive torso momentum, or rounding the lower back; reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A seated cable row using a wide grip attachment that emphasizes the upper back by driving the elbows outward to retract the shoulder blades. Performed seated with feet braced, it develops thicker upper-back musculature and posterior shoulder strength.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width using an overhand or mixed grip.
Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to set the upper back before the first rep.
Pull the barbell upward toward the lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and keeping them close to the body; focus on squeezing the upper back at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a flat back, then lower the barbell under control until arms are nearly extended while preserving the hip-hinge position.
Breathe out while pulling the bar up and inhale while lowering it; keep the torso stable and avoid rounding the lower back or using excessive torso momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, and reset your hip-hinge position between sets if you lose posture.
exercises.description: A hip-hinged barbell row that trains the upper back by pulling the loaded barbell toward the torso while maintaining a neutral spine. It also recruits the lats, arms and posterior chain for stability and power.

exercises.name: Barbell Behind The Back Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright in a stable stance (feet about hip-width) with a loaded barbell held behind your thighs using an overhand grip; hands should be roughly shoulder-width apart and arms straight.
instructions: Stand upright in a stable stance (feet about hip-width) with a loaded barbell held behind your thighs using an overhand grip; hands should be roughly shoulder-width apart and arms straight. Keep a slight bend in the knees, chest up, and core braced (neutral spine).
Maintain the bar close to your body and keep your arms straight (do not bend the elbows).
Exhale and lift your shoulders straight upward toward your ears by contracting the traps; avoid rolling or rotating the shoulders — move only at the shoulder girdle (scapular elevation).
Hold the top position for 1–2 seconds and actively squeeze the traps and levator scapulae.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position under control until the traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining a neutral spine, tight core, and straight arms; adjust grip width or load as needed to maintain proper form.
exercises.description: Standing barbell shrug with the bar held behind the body; raise the shoulders straight up toward the ears to emphasize the upper trapezius/neck region, pause, then lower back down under control. The behind-the-back position slightly changes shoulder/scapular mechanics compared with front shrugs.

exercises.name: Cable Wide Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
Select an appropriate weight and grasp the wide bar with a pronated (overhand) grip wider than shoulder width.
Sit tall with a slight natural arch in the lower back, feet flat, chest up and head neutral.
Begin the rep by inhaling, then exhale as you pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back toward your ribcage.
Lead the motion with your elbows and squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom, bringing the bar to the upper chest or collarbone level.
Pause briefly while maintaining tension in the lats, keeping the torso relatively upright and avoiding excessive backward lean or swinging.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the start position with controlled extension of the arms until you feel a full stretch in the lats.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and controlled breathing throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable wide-grip lat pulldown using an overhand wide bar to pull the cable down toward the upper chest, emphasizing shoulder adduction and scapular depression to work the latissimus dorsi. Performed seated with knees braced to isolate the back and minimize leg drive.

exercises.name: Cable one arm lat pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads.
Attach a single D-handle to the high pulley. Sit upright with a slight natural arch in the lower back and grasp the handle with one hand using a neutral or overhand grip.
Start with your working arm fully extended overhead and your torso upright (a very slight lean back is optional), opposite hand resting on your thigh or the machine for stability.
Exhale and pull the handle straight down and slightly toward the side of your ribcage by driving your elbow down and back; lead the movement with the elbow rather than the hand.
At the bottom of the movement, pause and squeeze the lat and upper-back muscles, keeping the shoulder blade pulled down and back; avoid shrugging the shoulder up.
Inhale and slowly return the handle to the start position with a controlled motion, allowing the lat to stretch but maintaining tension in the cable.
Perform the desired number of reps, then switch sides. Avoid twisting the torso or using momentum — keep the movement controlled and driven by the lat.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable pulldown performed from a seated position to isolate each lat muscle while limiting trunk rotation. The exercise pulls a handle from overhead down toward the side of the chest, emphasizing a strong squeeze of the lats.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at the hips and lean forward slightly so your torso is at about a 30–45° angle (standing, hip-hinged position).
Grab the bar with an overhand grip hands shoulder-width apart and extend your arms fully in front of you so the cable is taut; maintain a small, natural bend in the elbows (do not hyperextend).
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to engage the lats before initiating the movement.

Keeping the arms straight (maintaining the slight elbow bend), drive the bar down in an arc toward the thighs by extending the shoulder (pulling the upper arms down and back) until the bar reaches hip or upper-thigh level.
Pause briefly at the bottom and squeeze the lats and upper back while maintaining a stable, braced core and neutral spine (avoid rounding or excessive lumbar extension).
Slowly return the bar to the start position with control, allowing the shoulders to flex while maintaining tension in the lats; repeat for desired reps.

Common form tips: keep the torso still (avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean), maintain a slight elbow bend, and focus on scapular depression and lat contraction rather than pulling with the forearms.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-arm pulldowns hinge the torso forward and pull a high-set cable down with mostly straight arms to target the latissimus dorsi. The movement emphasizes scapular depression and shoulder extension while the core stabilizes the body.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Back Seated Row
exercises.notes: Set your spine in a neutral (straight) position with chest up and shoulders relaxed—avoid rounding the back or excessive leaning.
instructions: Sit on the bench with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent; grasp the close/V-handle with both hands and sit tall.
Set your spine in a neutral (straight) position with chest up and shoulders relaxed—avoid rounding the back or excessive leaning.
Begin the repetition by pulling your shoulder blades back and down (scapular retraction) to initiate the movement.
Continue pulling the handle toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows straight back; keep elbows close to the body.
Squeeze the lats and upper back at the end range for 1 second while maintaining an upright seated position (do not hyperextend the lower back).
Return the handle slowly under control by extending the arms and allowing the scapulae to protract until arms are nearly straight.
Breathe out as you pull, inhale as you return; use a controlled tempo and avoid using torso momentum to complete the reps.
exercises.description: Seated cable row performed with a straight, neutral spine; pull the handle toward the torso using shoulder blade retraction to emphasize the lats and upper back. Maintain an upright torso and controlled tempo throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Cable Front Seated Row
exercises.notes: Adjust the cable pulley to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the cable pulley to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
Sit on the bench facing the machine with your chest pressed against the vertical support pad, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90°.
Reach forward and grasp the handle with a neutral or overhand grip, arms fully extended and shoulders relaxed.
Keep your torso upright and core braced; maintain contact with the chest pad throughout the movement.
Exhale and pull the handle straight back by driving your elbows toward your ribcage, leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the end range when your hands reach your torso and you feel a full contraction across the upper back.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms forward in a controlled manner until they are nearly straight, resisting the weight on the return.
Repeat for the desired reps, avoiding trunk rotation, excessive trunk lean, or shrugging the shoulders; keep the motion controlled and driven by the back, not the arms.
exercises.description: A seated cable row variation performed facing the machine with chest supported by a pad, pulling a single handle straight toward the torso to work the mid/upper back. Emphasis is on scapular retraction and elbow drive to target the upper back and lats.

exercises.name: Cable Reverse Grip Pulldown
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate weight, then reach up and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip, palms facing you.
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown bench with your feet flat on the floor and knees secured under the thigh pads.
Select an appropriate weight, then reach up and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip, palms facing you.
Sit upright with a slight lean back (about 10–20 degrees), chest up, and scapulae slightly retracted; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Inhale, brace your core, then exhale and pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back until the bar reaches your upper chest or collarbone level.
Squeeze your lats and upper back at the bottom of the motion while keeping wrists neutral and avoiding excessive neck or shoulder shrugging.
Slowly control the weight back to the start by allowing the arms to extend fully overhead while maintaining scapular tension (do not fully relax shoulders between reps).
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out on the pull and inhale on the return, and avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean.
exercises.description: A seated cable pulldown performed with a supinated (reverse/underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while increasing biceps involvement. Pull the bar from an overhead position down to the upper chest while keeping the torso stable and shoulders retracted.

exercises.name: Cable Neutral Grip Wide Pulldown
exercises.notes: Reach up and grasp the wide neutral/V-bar handles with a palms-facing (neutral) grip, hands placed wide on the bar.
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown bench with knees secured under the pads, feet flat on the floor and select an appropriate weight.
Reach up and grasp the wide neutral/V-bar handles with a palms-facing (neutral) grip, hands placed wide on the bar.
Start with arms fully extended overhead, chest lifted and a slight controlled lean back (about 10–20°); keep the spine neutral.
Exhale and pull the handle down by driving your elbows down and back toward your sides, aiming to bring the handle to your upper chest/collarbone area.
Squeeze the lats and pinch the shoulder blades together at the bottom of the movement; keep wrists neutral and avoid shrugging the shoulders.
Pause briefly, then inhale and slowly return the handle to the start position under control until the arms are nearly straight, maintaining tension in the lats.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, avoiding excessive torso momentum or pulling the bar behind the neck.
exercises.description: A seated cable pulldown performed with a neutral (palms-facing) wide grip that emphasizes the latissimus dorsi while also engaging the upper back and arm muscles. The handle is pulled down to the upper chest while keeping the torso stable and focusing on elbow-driven movement.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at the hips and lean forward slightly so your torso is at about a 30–45° angle (standing, hip-hinged position).
Grab the bar with an overhand grip hands shoulder-width apart and extend your arms fully in front of you so the cable is taut; maintain a small, natural bend in the elbows (do not hyperextend).
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to engage the lats before initiating the movement.

Keeping the arms straight (maintaining the slight elbow bend), drive the bar down in an arc toward the thighs by extending the shoulder (pulling the upper arms down and back) until the bar reaches hip or upper-thigh level.
Pause briefly at the bottom and squeeze the lats and upper back while maintaining a stable, braced core and neutral spine (avoid rounding or excessive lumbar extension).
Slowly return the bar to the start position with control, allowing the shoulders to flex while maintaining tension in the lats; repeat for desired reps.

Common form tips: keep the torso still (avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean), maintain a slight elbow bend, and focus on scapular depression and lat contraction rather than pulling with the forearms.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-arm pulldowns hinge the torso forward and pull a high-set cable down with mostly straight arms to target the latissimus dorsi. The movement emphasizes scapular depression and shoulder extension while the core stabilizes the body.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment and select a light-to-moderate weight that allows strict form.
instructions: Set a high pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment and select a light-to-moderate weight that allows strict form.

Stand facing the machine about 1–2 feet away, feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent; hinge forward at the hips so your torso is angled forward (standing bent-over position) with a neutral spine.
Reach up and grasp the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down) about shoulder-width apart; maintain a small, natural bend in the elbows throughout (do not lock).
Brace your core and depress your shoulder blades slightly (avoid shrugging) so tension is felt in the lats before starting the pull.

Exhale and pull the bar down in a controlled arc by extending the shoulders and pulling with the lats until the bar reaches your upper thighs or mid-thigh level; keep the arms long and the elbows fixed in their slight bend.
Squeeze the lats and hold the bottom position briefly while keeping the ribs down and spine neutral.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the start position under control, resisting the cable so the lats remain under tension; do not let the lower back round as you return.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining the hip hinge, neutral spine, and long-arm position on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-arm pulldowns hinge from the hips and pull a high cable down with mostly straight arms to load the lats through a long-range extension. The movement emphasizes scapular depression and shoulder extension while keeping the torso braced.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright (feet about hip-width), hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at your sides, knees softly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright (feet about hip-width), hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at your sides, knees softly bent and core braced.
Keep your arms straight (no shrugging with the elbows) and maintain a neutral spine and head position throughout.
Exhale and lift (shrug) both shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as you can, focusing on contracting the top of the traps.
Pause and squeeze at the top for 1–2 seconds, avoiding rolling or rotating the shoulders.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position with control until arms are fully relaxed at your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping movement vertical and controlled; increase weight only if you can maintain form.
exercises.description: Standing exercise using dumbbells to raise the shoulders toward the ears, primarily working the trapezius muscles to develop upper-back and neck strength. Performed with a short vertical shrugging motion while keeping the arms straight.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Full squat (with rack)
exercises.notes: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
instructions: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
Stand under the bar with it resting across your upper back/traps (not on the neck). Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width.
Unrack the bar by straightening your legs to lift it off the hooks, then take 1–2 small steps back so the rack is clear; stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Brace your core (tighten abs), keep chest up and eyes forward. Inhale and begin the descent by pushing hips back and bending knees, keeping knees tracking over toes.
Lower to full depth (hips at or below parallel to the knees) while maintaining a neutral spine and weight distributed through the mid-foot and heels.
Drive through the heels and mid-foot, exhale, and extend hips and knees to return to a fully upright standing position while maintaining a tight core and upright torso.
Step forward and carefully re-rack the bar on the hooks. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat performed from a rack where the lifter unracks the bar, steps back and squats to full depth (hips at or below parallel) before returning to standing. This compound lower-body movement primarily loads the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart with the barbell over the mid-foot and toes pointing slightly out.
Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
Grip the bar just outside your knees with a double overhand or mixed grip; set shoulders slightly in front of the bar and keep the bar close to your shins.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, keep a neutral (flat) spine and chest lifted — avoid rounding the back.
Drive through the heels and simultaneously extend hips and knees, keeping the bar close to your body and the torso angle changing minimally at first.
Stand tall at the top by finishing hip extension and squeezing the glutes; do not hyperextend the lower back — shoulders should be back but not excessively retracted.
To descend, hinge at the hips first by pushing them back, then bend the knees once the bar passes them, lowering the bar under control to the floor while maintaining a neutral spine.
Reset your breath and core brace at the bottom for the next repetition; maintain consistent foot placement, bar path, and posture throughout the set.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge strength exercise that lifts a loaded barbell from the floor by extending the hips and knees; it targets the posterior chain with heavy involvement of the hamstrings and glutes while the back and core stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Barbell Floor Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Load a barbell and position it across the upper back/trapezius, not the neck; grip the bar just outside shoulder width with a firm hold.
instructions: Load a barbell and position it across the upper back/trapezius, not the neck; grip the bar just outside shoulder width with a firm hold.
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and weight evenly distributed on the mid-foot; keep a slight bend in the knees (not locked).
Brace your core and maintain an upright torso; focus on keeping shoulders down and the bar stable on your traps/delts.
Slowly raise both heels by pushing through the balls of your feet until you reach full plantarflexion (stand on your toes); contract the calves at the top and hold for 1–2 seconds.
Lower your heels back to the floor in a controlled manner until the soles are flat, feeling a stretch in the calves.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the ascent and inhaling on the descent.
If balance is an issue, perform the movement near a wall or use a spotter; avoid bouncing or using excessive forward trunk lean to lift the weight.
exercises.description: Standing barbell calf raise performed with a barbell resting on the upper back to load the lower legs; raise the heels by plantarflexing the ankles, pause at the top, then lower with control. This exercise primarily isolates the gastrocnemius/soleus (calves) while the upper back and shoulders stabilize the load.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Full Squat
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
instructions: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
Grip the bar just outside shoulder width, lift the chest, pull the shoulder blades together, and unrack the bar by straightening the legs and stepping back to clear the rack.
Position feet shoulder-width to slightly wider with toes pointed slightly outward; establish a stable stance with weight distributed through the midfoot and heel.
Brace your core (abs) and keep a neutral spine; inhale before you descend to maintain intra-abdominal pressure.
Begin the descent by pushing the hips back and bending the knees simultaneously, keeping the chest up and spine neutral — descend until your hips drop below parallel (full squat depth).
Ensure knees track over the toes and do not collapse inward; keep the weight on the heels/midfoot and maintain even foot contact with the floor.
Pause briefly at the bottom if desired, then drive forcefully through the heels to extend the hips and knees and return to a fully upright position, exhaling as you ascend.
Control the movement on the way up and down; use safety pins or a spotter when training heavy and avoid rounding the lower back at any point.
exercises.description: A barbell back full squat performed to below-parallel depth; primarily loads the quadriceps while also recruiting the glutes, hamstrings and core for stabilization. The lifter descends with the bar on the upper back and then drives back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Stiff Legged Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar just outside your legs with an overhand or mixed grip.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar just outside your legs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Set a slight, fixed bend in the knees (stiff-legged, not fully locked) and brace your core so the spine is neutral; chest up and shoulders back.
Initiate the rep by hinging at the hips: push your hips straight back while keeping the bar close to your legs and maintaining a long, flat back.
Lower the bar in a controlled manner until you feel a deep stretch in the hamstrings (bar will be around mid-shin or just below the knees depending on mobility); do not let the lower back round.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes to return to a standing position while keeping the slight knee bend.
Exhale as you reach the top, maintain control on the descent, and repeat for the desired number of reps.
Safety notes: reduce range of motion or weight if your lower back rounds, keep the neck neutral (look a few feet ahead), and use straps or lighter loads if grip (forearms) fails before hamstrings.
exercises.description: A standing hip-hinge movement performed with a barbell and minimal knee bend that lengthens and loads the hamstrings while also engaging the glutes and lower back. Keep a neutral spine and the bar close to the legs throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Landmine Rear Lunge
exercises.notes: Setup: Secure one end of the barbell in a landmine attachment.
instructions: Setup: Secure one end of the barbell in a landmine attachment. Stand facing the bar with feet about hip-width apart and the working leg slightly forward. Grasp the free end of the bar with both hands close to the chest in a goblet-style hold (elbows tucked).
Brace your core and stand tall with a neutral spine and shoulders down and back. Keep weight distributed through the midfoot and heel of the front foot.

Step the non-working leg straight back onto the toes (standing on the ball of the back foot) and lower into a controlled rear lunge by bending both knees until the front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor or the back knee approaches the ground.

Keep the torso upright (slight forward lean allowed), chest proud, and the front knee tracking over the toes without collapsing inward. Maintain a firm grip on the bar against the chest throughout the movement.
Drive through the front heel and contract the glutes to push yourself back up to the starting standing position while bringing the back foot forward.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then switch legs. Breathe in while lowering and exhale while driving back up.

Coaching tips: Use a controlled tempo, avoid letting the front knee travel excessively past the toes, and reduce weight if balance or form breaks down. Keep the elbow position stable to avoid undue shoulder strain.
exercises.description: A unilateral loaded lunge performed with a landmine bar held at the chest that emphasizes hip extension to drive the back leg and load the glutes while the front leg controls descent and ascent. It challenges single-leg strength, balance, and core stability.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
instructions: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
Unlatch or release the safety so the weight rests on the shoulders and stand tall with a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (not locked).
Inhale, then exhale as you press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, squeezing the calves at the top.
Hold the top position briefly (1 second) while maintaining an upright torso and stable hips.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves.
Control the descent—avoid bouncing—and repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack or secure the lever when finished.
exercises.description: A bilateral standing calf raise performed on a lever/shoulder machine that isolates the calf muscles through plantarflexion. The exercise raises the heels off a platform, then lowers them below platform level for a full stretch.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Ipsilateral Single Leg Stiff Leg Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright on one leg (working leg) with a dumbbell held in the hand on the same side as the standing leg (ipsilateral).
instructions: Stand upright on one leg (working leg) with a dumbbell held in the hand on the same side as the standing leg (ipsilateral). Keep the non-working foot lightly off the floor or resting just behind the heel for balance.
Set a small, controlled bend in the knee of the working leg and brace your core so your spine remains neutral; keep shoulders back and the chest long.

Hinge at the hips by pushing them straight back while maintaining the slight knee bend; allow the dumbbell to lower straight down the front of the working leg toward the mid-shin while the non-working leg extends straight back to counterbalance.
Keep hips square to the floor and avoid rotating the torso; maintain a neutral neck by looking at a point on the floor a few feet in front of you.
Lower until you feel a strong but controlled stretch in the hamstrings (torso approx. parallel to the floor or limited by flexibility), then pause briefly.
Drive the hips forward, squeezing the hamstrings and glutes to return to the upright start position while exhaling; keep the movement slow and controlled throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides so the dumbbell is held in the opposite hand on the opposite standing leg.
Breathing and tempo: inhale on the descent, exhale on the ascent; maintain control to protect the lower back and prioritize balance.
exercises.description: A single-leg Romanian (stiff-leg) deadlift performed while holding a dumbbell on the same side as the standing leg; it emphasizes a hip hinge to load the hamstrings and glutes while challenging single-leg balance and core stability.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backrest so your hips and knees align with the machine's pivot; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and backrest so your hips and knees align with the machine's pivot; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back fully supported against the back pad (seated position) and grip the handles for stability.
Position the lower leg pad just above your heels / at the lower calf (behind the ankles) and secure the thigh pad across your front upper thighs to prevent hip movement.
Start with your legs nearly extended (knees close to straight but not locked) and feet pointed forward.
Exhale and curl your lower legs downward/back by flexing the knees, bringing the pad toward the underside of your thighs; contract the hamstrings and glutes at the peak.
Hold the peak contraction briefly, then inhale and slowly extend your knees to return the weight to the start position with controlled tempo—do not let the stack slam or lock the knees.
Keep your hips and torso stable against the pad throughout; avoid lifting the hips or using momentum to move the weight.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions and adjust weight if you cannot maintain smooth, controlled reps.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl performed on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to target the hamstrings while the seat/back pad stabilizes the hips. The movement curls the lower leg back toward the underside of the thigh and then returns under control to the start.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Full squat (with rack)
exercises.notes: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
instructions: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
Stand under the bar with it resting across your upper back/traps (not on the neck). Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width.
Unrack the bar by straightening your legs to lift it off the hooks, then take 1–2 small steps back so the rack is clear; stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Brace your core (tighten abs), keep chest up and eyes forward. Inhale and begin the descent by pushing hips back and bending knees, keeping knees tracking over toes.
Lower to full depth (hips at or below parallel to the knees) while maintaining a neutral spine and weight distributed through the mid-foot and heels.
Drive through the heels and mid-foot, exhale, and extend hips and knees to return to a fully upright standing position while maintaining a tight core and upright torso.
Step forward and carefully re-rack the bar on the hooks. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat performed from a rack where the lifter unracks the bar, steps back and squats to full depth (hips at or below parallel) before returning to standing. This compound lower-body movement primarily loads the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lunge
exercises.notes: Stand tall in a neutral, upright position holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip), feet hip-width apart.
instructions: Stand tall in a neutral, upright position holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip), feet hip-width apart.
Brace your core (abs) and keep your chest up and shoulders back throughout the movement.
Take a controlled step forward about 2–3 feet with one leg so you can lower into a comfortable lunge (adjust distance for your limb length).
Lower your body by bending both knees until the front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor and the rear knee is just above the ground; aim for about a 90° angle at the front knee.
Keep the front knee tracking over the toes (do not let it collapse inward) and avoid letting the front knee travel excessively past the toes.
Press through the front heel and drive the hip forward to return to the starting standing position, squeezing the glutes at the top.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch legs (or perform alternating lunges) maintaining steady tempo and controlled movement.
Breathe in as you lower, exhale as you push back up; use a weight that allows good form and full range of motion.
exercises.description: A weighted forward lunge performed standing while holding dumbbells at your sides; it emphasizes the front thigh (quadriceps) while also recruiting the glutes and hamstrings for hip drive and calves/abs for stability.

exercises.name: Lever Glute Press
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine to the desired weight and adjust the chest pad height so your torso is upright and supported when gripping the handles.
instructions: Set the lever machine to the desired weight and adjust the chest pad height so your torso is upright and supported when gripping the handles.
Stand facing the machine with your chest pressed into the pad, both hands holding the handles for support, and feet on the platform or footpads as required by the machine.
Position the working leg so the foot is centered on the footplate with the knee slightly bent (single‑leg variation) or place both feet if using both legs; keep the non‑working leg clear of the path.
Brace your core (abs) and maintain a neutral spine and fixed chest on the pad; avoid leaning forward or arching the lower back.
Drive the working foot backward and slightly upward by extending the hip, contracting the glute until the leg is nearly straight or you reach a controlled end range of hip extension.
Pause briefly at full contraction, squeezing the glute, then slowly return the lever to the start position with control, resisting the weight rather than letting it snap back.
Complete the prescribed reps, then switch legs and repeat. Keep movement smooth and controlled throughout; reduce range or weight if you feel lumbar strain.
exercises.description: A chest‑supported, single‑leg hip extension performed on a lever machine that drives the footplate backward to extend the hip and contract the glute. It develops unilateral hip power and isolates the glute while the core stabilizes the torso.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright near the edge of a flat bench with knees bent at about 90 degrees and feet flat on the floor (or on a small block for increased range of motion).
instructions: Sit upright near the edge of a flat bench with knees bent at about 90 degrees and feet flat on the floor (or on a small block for increased range of motion).
Place a barbell across your upper thighs just above the knees; use a pad if needed for comfort and grip the bar with both hands to steady it (seated position).
Position the balls of your feet on the floor (or a small raised platform) while keeping heels hanging or supported so your ankles can move through full range.
Keeping your torso upright and core engaged, exhale and press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, squeezing the calves at the top.

Pause briefly at the top with the calves fully contracted, then inhale and slowly lower the heels back down until you feel a stretch in the calves but maintain control (avoid letting the weight slam down).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; maintain a steady tempo, avoid bouncing, and keep knees stationary throughout the set.
To stop, carefully roll the barbell forward off your thighs or have a spotter help lift it off before standing.
exercises.description: Seated on a bench with a loaded barbell resting across the thighs, raise the heels by plantarflexing the ankles to target the calf muscles. The movement is performed with knees bent, emphasizing the soleus portion of the calves through controlled heel raises and lowers.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the middle of your feet; grip the bar just outside your thighs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge) and allow the bar to descend close to your legs, keeping your chest up and eyes forward.
Continue hinging until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (bar typically reaches just below the knees or mid-shin depending on flexibility) while maintaining a flat back.
Pause briefly at the bottom without rounding the lower back; keep the lats engaged to keep the bar close to the body.
Drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to a standing position, finishing with hips fully extended but without hyperextending the spine.
Breathe: inhale on the descent, exhale as you drive the hips forward. Repeat for desired repetitions, focusing on controlled tempo and maintaining the hip-hinge pattern.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed standing with a barbell that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings and glutes while the posterior chain and grip stabilize the load. Maintain a neutral spine and a soft knee angle throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Stiff Leg Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, arms hanging in front of your thighs.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, arms hanging in front of your thighs.
Soften the knees slightly (small, fixed bend) and keep a neutral spine and head in line with the torso throughout the movement.
Initiate the movement by pushing the hips straight back (hip hinge), lowering the dumbbells down the front of the legs toward mid-shin while maintaining the slight knee bend.
Keep the dumbbells close to the legs, shoulders back and chest open — avoid rounding the lower back; stop when you feel a deep hamstring stretch or when your torso is near parallel to the floor.
Reverse the movement by driving the hips forward, engaging the hamstrings and glutes to stand back up while keeping the arms straight and the weights close to the body.
Squeeze the glutes at the top and maintain tight core/bracing; repeat for prescribed reps while controlling the eccentric (lowering) phase.
Progress by increasing weight gradually and prioritize form over load; avoid excessive knee bend or rounded spine to reduce injury risk.
exercises.description: A standing hip-hinge movement performed with dumbbells that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings while keeping the legs relatively straight. Lower the weights by pushing the hips back, then extend the hips to return to standing and squeeze the glutes.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Lever One Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat and back so your knee lines up with the machine's pivot point and the lower leg pad rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat and back so your knee lines up with the machine's pivot point and the lower leg pad rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
Sit upright with your back flat against the pad, chest up, and grip the side handles for stability; keep the non-working leg relaxed on the floor or slightly bent.
Start with the working knee bent to about 90 degrees (or slightly less) and the foot dorsiflexed (toes up) so the pad contacts above the ankle.
Exhale and extend the working knee by lifting the lower leg until it is almost straight; avoid fully locking the knee at the top.
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize quad contraction while maintaining a controlled posture (abs engaged, back against pad).
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with control, resisting the weight rather than letting it drop.
Repeat for the prescribed repetitions, then switch sides and perform the same steps for the other leg.
exercises.description: Seated single-leg extension on a lever machine that isolates and strengthens the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance. Performed one leg at a time to focus on each quad and correct imbalances.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Full Squat
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
instructions: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
Grip the bar just outside shoulder width, lift the chest, pull the shoulder blades together, and unrack the bar by straightening the legs and stepping back to clear the rack.
Position feet shoulder-width to slightly wider with toes pointed slightly outward; establish a stable stance with weight distributed through the midfoot and heel.
Brace your core (abs) and keep a neutral spine; inhale before you descend to maintain intra-abdominal pressure.
Begin the descent by pushing the hips back and bending the knees simultaneously, keeping the chest up and spine neutral — descend until your hips drop below parallel (full squat depth).
Ensure knees track over the toes and do not collapse inward; keep the weight on the heels/midfoot and maintain even foot contact with the floor.
Pause briefly at the bottom if desired, then drive forcefully through the heels to extend the hips and knees and return to a fully upright position, exhaling as you ascend.
Control the movement on the way up and down; use safety pins or a spotter when training heavy and avoid rounding the lower back at any point.
exercises.description: A barbell back full squat performed to below-parallel depth; primarily loads the quadriceps while also recruiting the glutes, hamstrings and core for stabilization. The lifter descends with the bar on the upper back and then drives back up to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lunge
exercises.notes: Start standing upright with feet hip-width apart, a dumbbell in each hand held at your sides, and core braced (neutral spine).
instructions: Start standing upright with feet hip-width apart, a dumbbell in each hand held at your sides, and core braced (neutral spine).
Take a controlled step forward with one leg, landing heel-first and keeping the feet roughly hip-width apart for balance.
Lower your body by bending both knees until the front thigh is about parallel to the floor and the back knee is close to the ground (but not resting on it).
Keep the torso upright and chest lifted, shoulders back, and ensure the front knee tracks in line with the foot (avoid collapsing inward or letting the knee travel far past the toes).
Drive through the front heel to push yourself back up to the starting standing position, fully extending the front hip and knee.
Repeat for the desired number of reps on that leg, then switch legs; alternatively perform alternating forward steps (walking lunges).
Maintain controlled tempo, steady breathing, and avoid leaning forward or rounding the lower back during the descent and ascent.
exercises.description: A weighted forward lunge performed while holding a dumbbell in each hand; the movement primarily loads the quadriceps while the glutes and hamstrings assist the drive and the core stabilizes the torso.

exercises.name: Smith One Leg Floor Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar at shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar at shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).

Stand with feet hip-width, unrack the bar and take a small step forward so the bar is balanced; shift your weight onto one foot and lift the other foot slightly off the floor or cross it behind the working leg.
Keep the working knee slightly bent (not fully locked) and maintain an upright torso with core engaged.
Exhale and press through the ball of the working foot to rise up as high as possible onto your toes (heel raise), focusing on contracting the calf.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and lower slowly back down with control until your heel returns to the floor (or to neutral dorsiflexion), keeping balance throughout.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then carefully re-rack the bar if switching legs, or step back under the bar and repeat for the opposite leg.
Maintain steady tempo, avoid bouncing, and keep shoulders and hips squared forward to prevent compensations.
exercises.description: A unilateral standing calf raise performed inside a Smith machine with the bar resting on the upper back; raises the body onto the ball of one foot to load the calf muscles. Performed on the floor (not a raised step) for balance and single-leg strength and stability.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
