exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height.
instructions: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height. Step under the bar and position it across the front of your shoulders (front of delts/upper chest) using either a clean (front-rack) grip or a cross-arm hold.
Stand up to unrack the bar, take one or two small steps back, and place your feet about shoulder-width apart with toes pointed slightly out.
Raise your elbows so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and brace your abs to keep the torso upright throughout the movement.
Inhale, push your hips back slightly and bend at the knees to descend into a squat; keep your chest up, elbows high, and knees tracking over your toes.
Continue lowering until your thighs reach at least parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows) while maintaining a neutral spine.
Exhale and drive through your heels and midfoot to extend your knees and hips, returning to the standing start position with control.
Maintain tight abs and high elbows on every rep; avoid letting the knees cave inward or the torso fold forward. Rack the bar safely when finished.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat where the barbell is held across the front of the shoulders, emphasizing upright torso and knee extension to target the quadriceps. Performed by descending into a squat and driving back up while keeping elbows high and core braced.

exercises.name: Sled 45 degrees Wide Stance Leg Press
exercises.notes: Set up the sled leg press: load appropriate weight, sit supine with your back fully supported on the 45-degree sled back pad, and grasp the side handles for stability.
instructions: Set up the sled leg press: load appropriate weight, sit supine with your back fully supported on the 45-degree sled back pad, and grasp the side handles for stability.

Place your feet high enough on the footplate with a wide stance (feet wider than shoulder-width) and toes slightly pointed outward to bias the glutes and adductors while keeping the knees tracking over the toes.
Release the safety latch or pins so the sled can move freely, keeping control with your hands on the handles.

Inhale and slowly lower the sled by bending at the knees and hips, lowering until your thighs are roughly parallel to the footplate or until you feel a comfortable stretch in the quads and glutes (avoid letting the lower back lift off the pad).
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the legs and ensuring knees remain aligned with the feet (do not let knees collapse inward).
Exhale and press through your heels and midfoot to extend the knees and hips, driving the sled back up to near full extension without locking out the knees.
Control the return motion and repeat for the desired number of reps, then carefully re-engage the safety latch before exiting the sled.
exercises.description: A 45-degree sled leg press performed with a wide foot placement to emphasize the quadriceps while engaging the glutes and inner thighs. The exerciser pushes the sled away by extending the knees and hips from a reclined (back-supported) position.

exercises.name: Smith Deadlift
exercises.notes: Set up the Smith machine: position safety stops at or just below mid-shin and load the desired weight; stand with feet roughly hip-width apart under the bar so the bar is over the mid-foot.
instructions: Set up the Smith machine: position safety stops at or just below mid-shin and load the desired weight; stand with feet roughly hip-width apart under the bar so the bar is over the mid-foot.

Stand facing the bar (upright standing position), bend at the hips and knees to reach the bar, keeping your chest up and spine neutral; grip the bar just outside the legs with a double overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull shoulders down and back (retract traps slightly) and ensure the bar is close to your shins with shoulders slightly in front of the bar before initiating the lift.
Drive through the heels by extending the hips and knees together, pushing the floor away while keeping a strong hip hinge; keep the bar close to the body along the fixed vertical path.
Continue to extend until hips are fully locked out with hips forward and chest upright; avoid hyperextending the spine at the top.
To lower, hinge at the hips first and push them back while maintaining a neutral spine, then bend the knees once the bar passes the knees and lower it under control to the starting position.
Breathe: inhale and brace before each rep, exhale at or after the top of the lift; use controlled tempo and avoid rounding the back.
Use the Smith machine safety stops for sets to limit range if needed and reduce weight if you cannot maintain neutral spine or bar path.
exercises.description: A barbell deadlift variation performed on a Smith machine that emphasizes the hip hinge to train the glutes and posterior chain while the fixed bar path adds stability and control. It develops hip extension strength and reinforces safe bar path for beginners or heavier loads.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Sled Calf Press On Leg Press
exercises.notes: Set up the leg press sled with an appropriate weight and unlock the safety catches.
instructions: Set up the leg press sled with an appropriate weight and unlock the safety catches.
Sit reclined with your back and head firmly against the pad (supine/reclined position) and place the balls of your feet and toes on the lower edge of the footplate so your heels can hang freely.
Keep your knees slightly bent (do not lock) and grip the handles or sides of the seat for stability.
Inhale, then exhale and press through the balls of your feet to lift the sled by plantarflexing your ankles, raising your heels as high as comfortable.
Pause briefly at the top for a full contraction of the calves.
Slowly lower your heels back down below the level of the footplate to achieve a controlled stretch of the calves, keeping the motion smooth and under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then carefully re-engage the safety catches before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: Performed on a leg press sled while reclined, this exercise isolates ankle plantarflexion by pressing through the balls of the feet to raise the sled using the calf muscles. It emphasizes a full range of motion from a stretch (heels lowered) to a strong contraction (heels lifted).

exercises.name: Lever Lying Single Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the lever pad sits just above the back of your ankle and the pivot aligns with your knee joint.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the lever pad sits just above the back of your ankle and the pivot aligns with your knee joint.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with hips and pelvis firmly against the pad, one foot hooked under the padded lever and the other leg extended and relaxed.
Grasp the handles or edge of the bench for stability and brace your core to prevent your hips from lifting.
Start with the working leg nearly straight (slight natural bend in the knee) and exhale as you flex the knee to curl the heel toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause briefly at the top when you feel a full hamstring contraction without excessive hip extension.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start position, fully controlling the eccentric phase without letting the weight slam down.
Keep the non-working leg relaxed and hips pressed into the bench; avoid lifting or thrusting the hips to cheat the movement.
Complete the desired reps, then switch legs and repeat for the other side.
exercises.description: Prone (lying face down) single-leg curl performed on a lever/lying leg curl machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee to bring the heel toward the glutes. Movement emphasizes a controlled concentric curl and a slow eccentric return while keeping the hips pinned to the bench.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a front-rack position on a squat rack at about collarbone height; approach the bar and step under it so it rests on the anterior deltoids and across the clavicles in a cross-arm or clean grip.
instructions: Set the barbell in a front-rack position on a squat rack at about collarbone height; approach the bar and step under it so it rests on the anterior deltoids and across the clavicles in a cross-arm or clean grip.
Establish your grip (cross-arm shown in video) and lift the chest: elbows high and pointed forward to create a stable shelf for the bar; keep the upper back tight and shoulders engaged.
Unrack the bar by standing up and taking 1-2 steps back; position feet shoulder-width apart with toes turned slightly outward and weight distributed on the midfoot/heel.
Brace your core and inhale deeply to create intra-abdominal pressure; keep the chest up and eyes forward throughout the movement.
Initiate the squat by bending at the hips and knees simultaneously, sitting back slightly but maintaining an upright torso so the bar stays balanced over the mid-foot.

Descend until the thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or to a comfortable depth while maintaining an upright torso and neutral spine). Ensure knees track in line with the toes and do not cave inward.
Drive the movement upward by pressing through the heels and midfoot, extending the knees and hips while keeping the chest lifted and elbows high; exhale as you stand to full extension.
Reset your breath and posture between reps if needed; maintain controlled tempo and avoid letting the torso collapse forward or the heels rise.
After the set, carefully step forward and re‑rack the bar on the squat rack ensuring control and proper placement before fully releasing.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat variation that emphasizes the quadriceps by keeping the torso more upright and shifting load to the front of the legs; the bar is held in a front-rack or cross-arm position. It trains lower-body strength while also demanding core and upper-back stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Box Squat
exercises.notes: Set a sturdy box or bench behind you at about parallel (hip crease roughly level with or just above the top of the knee) and load a barbell on your upper back (high- or low-bar based on preference).
instructions: Set a sturdy box or bench behind you at about parallel (hip crease roughly level with or just above the top of the knee) and load a barbell on your upper back (high- or low-bar based on preference).
Stand with feet about shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward; grip the bar comfortably and brace your core, pulling your shoulder blades together to create a stable upper back.
Unrack the bar and take one or two small steps back to clear the rack, keeping chest up and eyes forward.

Begin the descent by pushing your hips back and bending at the knees simultaneously — sit back onto the box in a controlled manner, keeping weight through your heels and knees tracking over your toes.
Lightly contact the box without fully relaxing or bouncing; maintain core tension and a firm upper-back position while paused (brief 1–2 second hold).
Drive your feet into the floor and extend hips and knees to stand up explosively while exhaling, maintaining a neutral spine and upright chest.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Rack the bar safely or have a spotter present; use safety pins if available and choose a box height that allows safe, controlled depth.
exercises.description: A loaded squat variation where the lifter sits back onto a box before standing, emphasizing the thighs while engaging the posterior chain and core for stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Snatch
exercises.notes: Set the shoulders slightly in front of the bar, brace the abs, take a full breath and hold tension before initiating the lift.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip‑width, barbell over mid‑foot; take a wide snatch grip and hinge at the hips with knees bent so the bar is near the shins (upright torso slightly forward), back flat and chest up.
Set the shoulders slightly in front of the bar, brace the abs, take a full breath and hold tension before initiating the lift.
Begin the pull by driving through the heels and extending the hips and knees explosively (triple extension) to generate upward momentum on the bar; keep the bar close to the body.
As the hips extend, aggressively shrug the shoulders and pull the elbows up and out, keeping the bar close and the torso tall—arms remain largely straight until full extension.
At the top of the upward drive, rapidly pull yourself under the bar by rotating the wrists and pulling the elbows down so the bar can be received overhead with straight arms.
Catch the bar locked out overhead while dropping into a deep overhead squat (full squat position) with hips below parallel if mobility allows; ensure knees track toes and core is braced.
Stand up from the deep overhead squat to full hip and knee extension while maintaining a locked overhead position and stable balance.
Lower the bar under control to the back, front, or to the floor (depending on gym rules and skill level) before repeating or rest between attempts.
exercises.description: The barbell snatch is an explosive full‑body Olympic lift that moves the barbell from the floor to an overhead locked position in one continuous motion, finishing in a deep overhead squat. It emphasizes powerful hip extension and coordination to generate upward momentum and then rapidly drop under the bar to catch it overhead.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Squat Calf Raise on Leg Press Machine
exercises.notes: Sit back in the leg press seat with your back firmly against the pad and both hands holding the side handles for stability (seated, slightly reclined position).
instructions: Sit back in the leg press seat with your back firmly against the pad and both hands holding the side handles for stability (seated, slightly reclined position).
Place the balls of your feet on the lower edge of the footplate so your heels can move freely; feet should be hip-width and toes pointing forward.
Unlock or release the lever/safety according to the machine's instructions so the plate can move freely (machine-specific release), keeping control of the sled at all times.
Start with knees bent (seated squat position) and heels dropped to create a full stretch in the calves.
Drive through the balls of your feet, lifting your heels as high as possible by plantarflexing the ankles; squeeze the calves at the top of the movement.
Lower your heels slowly back to the starting stretched position under control — do not let the weight slam down.
Keep your knees slightly bent throughout to minimize knee extension and focus tension on the calves; avoid locking the knees.
Complete the desired reps, then return the sled to the safety catch before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: Seated on a lever-style leg press, the exercise isolates the calves by raising the heels against the footplate while the knees remain bent in a squat-like position. It emphasizes concentric calf contraction with a controlled eccentric stretch.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Straight Legs Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand, arms straight and palms facing the body.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand, arms straight and palms facing the body.
Soften the knees slightly (do not lock them) and set a neutral spine by bracing the abs and pulling the shoulder blades back slightly.
Initiate the movement by pushing the hips straight back (hip hinge), keeping the dumbbells close to the front of the thighs.
Lower the dumbbells down the front of your legs until you feel a strong but controlled stretch in the hamstrings (around mid-shin or just below the knees), maintaining a flat back and neutral neck.
Keep the knees only slightly bent throughout; avoid excessive knee bend or letting the lower back round.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to standing while keeping the dumbbells close to the legs.
Maintain a firm grip so the forearms remain engaged, exhale as you stand, and repeat for the desired reps.
Use lighter weights or reduce range of motion if you cannot maintain a neutral spine or hamstring tension throughout the set.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed with dumbbells that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings while also engaging the glutes and lower back; performed with nearly straight legs and a neutral spine. Keep the weights close to the legs and drive the hips back and forward to move the load.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft knees).
instructions: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft knees).
Brace your core, retract your shoulder blades slightly, and keep a neutral spine and chin tuck throughout the movement.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge), keeping the dumbbells close to your legs as you lower them down the front of your thighs.
Lower until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings or the dumbbells reach just below mid-shin, maintaining the slight knee bend and neutral spine — avoid rounding the back.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to standing, keeping the dumbbells close to the body.
Squeeze the glutes at the top without overextending the lower back and maintain controlled tempo; repeat for desired reps.
Coaching tips: keep weight in the heels, avoid bending excessively at the knees (this becomes a squat), and maintain a firm grip to engage the forearms.
exercises.description: A standing hip-hinge exercise performed with dumbbells that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the posterior chain, primarily the hamstrings, while maintaining a neutral spine. The movement lowers the weights by pushing the hips back and returns by driving the hips forward.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Single Leg Curl (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set up the machine by loading plates and adjusting the lower leg/roller pad so it sits just above the back of your ankle when standing.
instructions: Set up the machine by loading plates and adjusting the lower leg/roller pad so it sits just above the back of your ankle when standing.

Stand facing the machine, place your chest/abdomen against the support pad if provided, and grasp the handles or frame for stability; support your weight on the non-working foot while the working foot is behind the roller.
Start with the working leg almost fully extended (knee nearly straight) and maintain an upright torso with a braced core and hips squared to the machine.
Exhale and flex the knee of the working leg, curling your heel toward your buttocks in a smooth, controlled motion until you reach peak contraction of the hamstrings.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the hamstrings, avoiding excessive hip extension or swinging of the torso.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start position, controlling the resistance until the working leg is nearly straight without hyperextending the knee.
Perform the desired number of repetitions on one leg, then switch to the other leg; keep the non-working foot planted or lightly supported to maintain balance throughout.
Keep movement tempo controlled, avoid using momentum, and adjust load so you can maintain proper form for all reps.
exercises.description: A standing, single-leg hamstring curl performed on a plate-loaded lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee against resistance while supporting the body on the machine pad.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Feet Flat Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set the bench inside a rack so the bar sits at arm height when you lie down; load the bar and secure collars or have a spotter.
instructions: Set the bench inside a rack so the bar sits at arm height when you lie down; load the bar and secure collars or have a spotter.
Lie supine on the flat bench with your eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and planted for stability, and a slight natural arch in the lower back.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades (pinch them together and press them into the bench) to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the bar with a full grip slightly wider than shoulder width, wrists stacked over forearms and elbows under the bar.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar into the start position directly above your shoulders.
Inhale, then lower the bar in a controlled manner to mid-chest / nipple line, keeping elbows at about a 45° angle to the torso and maintaining scapular retraction.
Briefly pause with the bar lightly contacting the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward along a slightly arced path back to full arm extension.
Lock out the elbows at the top without overextending, keep the feet planted and core braced throughout the set.
After the final rep, carefully rack the bar onto the supports or have your spotter assist; use a spotter for heavy loads and maintain strict control on every rep.
exercises.description: Barbell feet-flat flat bench press is a supine pressing exercise that primarily works the pectorals by pressing a loaded barbell from the chest to full arm extension. Keeping the feet flat provides a stable base while the triceps and delts assist in elbow extension and shoulder stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Wide Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench under a rack and load the barbell; position yourself lying supine on the bench with eyes roughly under the bar.
instructions: Set up a flat bench under a rack and load the barbell; position yourself lying supine on the bench with eyes roughly under the bar.
Plant feet firmly on the floor, pull your shoulder blades together and down (retract and depress) and maintain a tight core and neutral spine.
Grip the bar with a wide, pronated grip (wider than shoulder-width) so the forearms are more angled when the bar is at chest level.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over your chest, keeping shoulders stable and the elbows under the bar.
Inhale, slowly lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest / nipple line while keeping the elbows tracking out at a moderate flare and the scapulae retracted.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar back up along a slight arc until the arms are nearly extended.
Avoid locking the elbows aggressively at the top, maintain tension in the pectorals and triceps, and keep the wrists neutral.
Return the bar to the rack with control after your set; use a spotter if lifting heavy or approaching failure.
exercises.description: A barbell bench press performed with a wider-than-shoulder grip to emphasize the outer pectoral fibers. Lying supine on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the mid-chest and pressed back to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.

Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.
instructions: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.
Lie supine on the incline bench with your head supported, feet flat on the floor, and back slightly arched while maintaining scapular retraction.
Grip the barbell with a pronated grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over your upper chest/shoulder line, then stabilize before starting the descent.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar in a controlled path to the upper chest/clavicular area, keeping elbows at roughly a 30–60° angle from the torso (avoid flaring to 90°).
Lightly touch or stop just above the chest, pause briefly without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward and slightly back to the start position, fully extending the elbows.
Keep core braced and feet planted throughout to maintain stability and prevent excessive lower back extension.
Carefully re-rack the bar under control or have a spotter assist when finished.
exercises.description: A barbell press performed on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest by pressing the bar upward and slightly toward the clavicle. It also engages the front delts and triceps for pressing and locking out the weight.

instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Low Fly
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell by each knee.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell by each knee.
Lie back so your head, upper back and glutes are supported (supine on incline) and plant your feet flat on the floor for stability.
Pick up the dumbbells and bring them to your sides (low position) with palms facing each other and a slight bend in the elbows; retract the shoulder blades slightly to protect the shoulders.
Initiate the movement by squeezing the pectorals and lifting the dumbbells in a wide arc toward each other above the upper chest, keeping the same slight elbow bend throughout.
Pause briefly at the top with the dumbbells close but not forcefully smashed together; exhale during the concentric (lifting) phase.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back down along the same arc to the low starting position under control, inhaling during the descent and maintaining tension in the chest.
Keep the torso stable (avoid excessive lumbar arching or using leg drive) and limit range if you feel shoulder pain; perform all reps with controlled tempo.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while focusing on chest contraction rather than moving the weight with momentum.
exercises.description: Lying on an incline bench, perform a dumbbell fly from a low starting position to emphasize the upper chest (pectorals) by arcing the arms upward and together. Keep a small bend in the elbows and control the descent to maintain tension on the chest.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Hammer Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and select two dumbbells of appropriate weight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and select two dumbbells of appropriate weight.
Sit on the bench and plant your feet flat on the floor; lie back so your head, upper back and hips are supported (supine on an incline bench).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing each other) grip; bring the dumbbells to shoulder level with elbows bent (~90°) and wrists stacked over elbows.
Brace your core, retract your shoulder blades slightly, and keep the elbows under the wrists (avoid excessive flaring).
Exhale and press both dumbbells up and slightly together until your arms are almost extended — do not lock the elbows at the top.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner back to shoulder level, keeping the same neutral grip.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while maintaining steady tempo, stable bench contact, and neutral wrist alignment.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, dumbbells are pressed upward with a neutral (hammer) grip to emphasize the upper chest. The movement uses a controlled press and descent to work the pectorals with assistance from the delts and triceps.

exercises.name: Assisted Parallel Close Grip Pull-up
exercises.notes: Set the assistance weight on the machine to provide the desired amount of help (more weight = easier). Train to muscular failure.

Start with your arms fully extended, chest lifted, shoulders pulled slightly down and back (scapular depression/retraction) and core braced.
Exhale and pull by driving your elbows down toward your ribs, squeezing your lats and upper back, until your chin clears the handles or your chest approaches the bar.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a tight shoulder position (avoid shrugging), then inhale and slowly lower yourself under control back to full arm extension.
Maintain a steady tempo, avoid swinging or kipping, and adjust assistance as you get stronger to progress the exercise.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Neutral grip Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach neutral handles (or a short rope) to each side. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach neutral handles (or a short rope) to each side.
Stand between the stacks in a staggered stance (one foot forward for balance) and take one handle in each hand with palms facing each other.
Step forward slightly until you feel tension in the cables, lean your torso forward a few degrees and keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout the movement.
Retract your shoulder blades slightly and brace your core to prevent trunk rotation or excessive arching.
Exhale and bring your hands together in a controlled horizontal arc in front of your chest, squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Hold the contracted position for 1–2 seconds, then inhale and slowly reverse the motion, letting the cables return your arms to the starting wide position while maintaining the elbow bend.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement slow and controlled and avoiding locking the elbows or using momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable fly using a neutral (palms-facing) grip that isolates the chest by bringing the hands together in a horizontal arc. Performed from a staggered stance with a slight elbow bend to emphasize the pectoral squeeze while the cables provide continuous tension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, keep a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (bent-over position).
Let your arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a slight bend in the elbows; maintain a braced core and avoid rounding the back.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the ground (level with the shoulders).
Squeeze the rear delts and shoulder blades together at the top for a brief pause, avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control, resisting any momentum; repeat for desired reps.
Keep the movement strict: use lighter weight if you must swing the torso or bend the elbows excessively, and breathe out on the lift and in on the lower.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell raise that isolates the rear (posterior) deltoids by lifting the arms out to the sides while the torso is hinged forward. It emphasizes shoulder horizontal abduction and scapular retraction with a controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Cable Reverse Grip Pulldown
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate weight, then reach up and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip, palms facing you. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown bench with your feet flat on the floor and knees secured under the thigh pads.
Select an appropriate weight, then reach up and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip, palms facing you.
Sit upright with a slight lean back (about 10–20 degrees), chest up, and scapulae slightly retracted; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Inhale, brace your core, then exhale and pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back until the bar reaches your upper chest or collarbone level.
Squeeze your lats and upper back at the bottom of the motion while keeping wrists neutral and avoiding excessive neck or shoulder shrugging.
Slowly control the weight back to the start by allowing the arms to extend fully overhead while maintaining scapular tension (do not fully relax shoulders between reps).
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out on the pull and inhale on the return, and avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean.
exercises.description: A seated cable pulldown performed with a supinated (reverse/underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while increasing biceps involvement. Pull the bar from an overhead position down to the upper chest while keeping the torso stable and shoulders retracted.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Lever One Arm Incline Chest Press (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine and add plates on the lever arm. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the lever machine and add plates on the lever arm. Adjust the seat height so the handle is roughly level with the upper chest/clavicular region when seated.

Sit with your back firmly against the incline pad, feet flat on the floor, and grasp the single handle with a firm grip using the working arm. Keep the non-working hand resting lightly on your thigh or the machine for balance.

Sit tall and brace your core; retract the shoulder blade of the working side slightly to create a stable chest position. Maintain a slight natural arch in the lower back but avoid leaning or twisting the torso.
Start with the elbow bent so the upper arm is at about shoulder height and the forearm is vertical. This is the start position.
Exhale and press the handle upward and slightly inward in a controlled arc until the arm is near full extension. Do not hyperextend or forcefully lock the elbow at the top.
Pause briefly at the top with control, then slowly inhale and lower the lever back along the same path until the upper arm returns to the start position and you feel a stretch in the chest.
Perform the desired number of reps for one arm while maintaining a stable torso and preventing rotation; then switch sides and repeat with the other arm.
Use a lighter weight when first learning unilateral control, focus on smooth tempo and full range of motion, and avoid bouncing or letting the shoulder collapse at the bottom of the movement.
exercises.description: A single-arm, plate-loaded incline press performed seated with back support that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest while allowing unilateral loading to correct imbalances and increase core stabilization.

exercises.name: Lever Assisted Standing Chin-up
exercises.notes: Set the machine assistance level (lighter assistance = harder) so you can perform controlled repetitions with good form. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the machine assistance level (lighter assistance = harder) so you can perform controlled repetitions with good form.
Stand on the platform facing the machine and grasp the handles with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width apart; arms should be fully extended at the start.
Keep feet planted on the platform, chest upright and core braced; avoid excessive swinging or leaning back.
Initiate the movement by pulling your elbows down and back, driving your chest toward the handles and squeezing the lats and upper back at the top of the motion.
Pause briefly with chin or upper chest close to the handles while maintaining scapular retraction and elbow flexion.
Slowly lower yourself back to the start position with controlled extension of the arms until they are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of reps and adjust assistance as needed to maintain strict form.
exercises.description: A lever-assisted, standing chin-up uses a counterbalance to reduce the effective bodyweight while performing an underhand pull, allowing you to train the vertical pulling pattern. It emphasizes the lats with strong involvement from the biceps and upper-back musculature for scapular retraction.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl to Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a neutral grip (palms facing inward). Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a neutral grip (palms facing inward).

Initiate a controlled dumbbell curl by rotating the palms to face upward and bending the elbows to bring the dumbbells up to shoulder level; keep elbows close to your sides and avoid swinging the torso.

At the top of the curl with dumbbells near shoulder height, rotate palms so they face forward and press the weights overhead by extending the elbows and driving through the shoulders until arms are fully extended.
Pause briefly at full extension with the ribs down and core engaged; avoid overarching the lower back.
Lower the dumbbells back down in a controlled motion to shoulder height, then reverse the curl movement to return the weights to your sides in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the press and inhaling as you lower; maintain a steady tempo and avoid using momentum from the hips or legs.
exercises.description: A standing combination exercise that starts with a dumbbell curl and transitions into an overhead press, targeting the shoulder muscles while also working the arms. Perform the curl to bring weights to shoulder level, then press the dumbbells overhead and control the descent back to the start.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench so the back pad is at a low angle and stand facing the side of the bench. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set an incline bench so the back pad is at a low angle and stand facing the side of the bench.
Pick up a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip and take a hip-width stance with a slight bend in the knees.
Hinge forward at the hips and place your forehead or chest gently against the bench pad for support so your torso is close to horizontal (bench-assisted bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a small bend in the elbows; keep a neutral spine and the head supported on the bench.
Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc — lead with the elbows — until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and you feel the rear delts contract.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting position.
Keep the movement strict (no torso rotation or momentum) and maintain head/chest support on the bench throughout the set.
Choose a weight that allows controlled form for the full range of motion and perform recommended reps.
exercises.description: A bent-over, bench-supported dumbbell raise that targets the rear (posterior) delts by moving the arms out to the sides while the torso is stabilized. The head/chest is supported on an incline bench to reduce momentum and isolate the rear deltoids.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Face Pull (with rope)
exercises.notes: Set a rope handle on a high pulley (about head/eye level) and select an appropriate weight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set a rope handle on a high pulley (about head/eye level) and select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright facing the machine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent (or seated on a bench); keep a tall spine and neutral head position.
Grasp the rope with a neutral grip (thumbs pointing toward you) and extend your arms so there is tension on the cable.
Initiate the movement by pulling the rope toward your face, leading with your elbows and keeping them at about shoulder height.
As you pull, externally rotate your shoulders so the ends of the rope separate and your hands move either side of your face; squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end range.
Pause briefly at peak contraction, keeping the torso stable and avoiding excessive backward lean or shrugging.
Slowly return the rope to the start position with control, maintaining tension in the cable and scapular control throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of reps and maintain controlled tempo to protect the shoulder joint.
exercises.description: A seated cable face pull using a rope attachment that emphasizes the rear delts and upper posterior shoulder musculature by pulling the rope toward the face while externally rotating the hands and squeezing the shoulder blades.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Incline Bench Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
Load a barbell and grasp it with a shoulder-width reverse (underhand) grip so palms face up and arms hang straight down toward the floor.
Start with your chest pressed into the bench, shoulders relaxed and scapulae slightly protracted, core engaged and neck neutral.

Initiate the movement by retracting your scapulae and pulling the barbell upward in a controlled path toward your lower chest / upper abdomen, leading with the elbows and keeping them close to your sides.
Pause 1 second at the top and squeeze the lats and upper back without hyperextending the spine or lifting your chest off the bench.
Lower the barbell slowly back to the start with full arm extension while maintaining tension in the lats; avoid dropping the weight or using momentum.
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out while pulling and inhale while lowering.
exercises.description: Prone incline bench row performed with a reverse (underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while also engaging the upper back and arms. Lying chest-down on an incline bench, you pull a barbell up toward the lower chest/upper abdomen and lower it under control.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width using an overhand or mixed grip.
Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to set the upper back before the first rep.
Pull the barbell upward toward the lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and keeping them close to the body; focus on squeezing the upper back at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a flat back, then lower the barbell under control until arms are nearly extended while preserving the hip-hinge position.
Breathe out while pulling the bar up and inhale while lowering it; keep the torso stable and avoid rounding the lower back or using excessive torso momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, and reset your hip-hinge position between sets if you lose posture.
exercises.description: A hip-hinged barbell row that trains the upper back by pulling the loaded barbell toward the torso while maintaining a neutral spine. It also recruits the lats, arms and posterior chain for stability and power.

exercises.name: Landmine Squat and Press
exercises.notes: Set up the landmine by securing one end of the barbell in the pivot and load the free end with appropriate weight; stand facing the bar with feet about shoulder‑width apart and toes slightly turned out.
instructions: Set up the landmine by securing one end of the barbell in the pivot and load the free end with appropriate weight; stand facing the bar with feet about shoulder‑width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Grip the end of the bar with both hands and hold it close to your chest in a front‑rack/goblet position (elbows tucked under the bar, forearms vertical) while standing tall and bracing your abs.
Begin the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees to lower into a controlled squat until your thighs are roughly parallel to the floor or a comfortable depth is reached.

Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand up explosively; as you reach near full hip and knee extension, press the bar upward and slightly forward using your delts and triceps until your arms are extended.
Pause briefly at the top with a stable torso, then slowly lower the bar back to the chest/front‑rack position under control and immediately move into the next squat.
Maintain a neutral spine, keep the chest up, breathe in before the descent and exhale during the press, and avoid letting the knees cave inwards throughout the movement.
exercises.description: A front‑loaded compound movement performed with a landmine bar that combines a squat with an overhead/forward press, loading the quads during the descent and the shoulders/triceps during the press. It trains lower‑body strength and upper‑body pressing in a single fluid motion.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Shoulders Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
Place the barbell in a front-rack position resting on the upper chest/clavicle area with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width and full grip around the bar.
Retract the shoulder blades slightly, keep the chest up and core braced (neutral spine); avoid excessive lumbar arching.
Take a full breath and begin the press by driving the bar upward in a straight line, slightly moving the head back as the bar passes and then returning the head forward under the bar.
Fully extend the elbows at the top with the bar directly over or slightly behind the head, shoulders stacked over hips and feet for balance.
Lower the bar under control back to the front-rack position to complete one rep while inhaling, keeping the elbows tracking slightly forward.
Repeat for desired repetitions, maintaining tight core engagement and controlled tempo; stop or reduce weight if you must excessively lean back to finish reps.
exercises.description: A standing barbell overhead press performed from the front-rack position that primarily develops the delts while requiring the triceps and upper back to assist and stabilize. The lifter presses the barbell from shoulder level to full arm extension overhead while maintaining a braced core.

exercises.name: Chin-Up
exercises.notes: Grasp a pull-up bar with a supinated (underhand) grip, hands about shoulder-width apart; hang from the bar with arms fully extended and feet off the ground (dead hang).
instructions: Grasp a pull-up bar with a supinated (underhand) grip, hands about shoulder-width apart; hang from the bar with arms fully extended and feet off the ground (dead hang).
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the torso before initiating the pull.
Drive your elbows down and back by contracting your biceps and lats, pulling your chest toward the bar.
Continue pulling until your chin clears the bar or the bar touches your upper chest, keeping the torso relatively upright and avoiding excessive swinging.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the working muscles (biceps and upper back).
Lower yourself slowly and with control back to the full hang, extending the arms fully while maintaining shoulder blade stability.
Breathe out during the ascent and inhale during the controlled descent.
If you cannot complete a full chin-up, use a band-assisted variation, a box/step for negative-only reps, or an assisted chin-up machine to reduce load while maintaining the same body position.
exercises.description: A vertical pulling exercise performed from a hanging position with a supinated (underhand) grip that primarily loads the biceps while also working the lats and upper back. Pull your chin over the bar and lower under control to build upper-body pulling strength.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Fly
exercises.notes: Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor (or a 30–45° decline), keep a neutral spine and neck, and brace your abs.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with feet flat about hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor (or a 30–45° decline), keep a neutral spine and neck, and brace your abs.
Allow the dumbbells to hang beneath your shoulders with a slight bend in the elbows; maintain that elbow angle throughout the set.

Exhale and lift the dumbbells out to your sides in a wide arc by externally rotating the shoulders and squeezing the shoulder blades together; raise until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Pause briefly at the top focusing on contracting the rear delts and upper back, then inhale and lower the weights slowly back to the starting position with control.
Avoid swinging the torso or using momentum, do not shrug the shoulders up toward the ears, and keep the movement driven by the shoulders/scapulae rather than the lower back.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell rear fly is a bent-over horizontal shoulder abduction movement that targets the rear delts and upper back by pulling the arms out to the sides while keeping the torso stable. It strengthens posterior shoulder muscles and scapular retractors with a controlled, rowing-like motion.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Pendlay Row
exercises.notes: Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; bend at the hips and knees to reach the bar so your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (hip hinge), knees slightly bent.
Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades down and back, then explosively row the bar in a straight line to your lower chest/upper abdomen, driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your upper back at the top for 1 second while keeping the torso stable (no rowing with the hips or excessive torso rotation).
Lower the bar under control all the way back to the floor so it comes to a complete stop, reset any slack, then repeat for the desired reps.
Maintain a flat back and braced core throughout; if your lower back rounds or you must use momentum, reduce weight or raise the bar height.
exercises.description: The Barbell Pendlay Row is a strict horizontal barbell row performed from a dead stop on the floor each rep, emphasizing the upper back and lats while minimizing torso momentum. It uses a hip hinge with a flat back and pulls the bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen before returning it to the floor.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.
