exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Chest Dip
exercises.notes: Starting position: Stand between parallel dip bars or on a dip station and grasp the handles with a firm neutral or slightly pronated grip; support your weight with straight arms and feet off the ground (legs can be bent at the knees or crossed behind you).
instructions: Starting position: Stand between parallel dip bars or on a dip station and grasp the handles with a firm neutral or slightly pronated grip; support your weight with straight arms and feet off the ground (legs can be bent at the knees or crossed behind you).
Adopt a slight forward lean from the hips so your chest moves ahead of your hands; keep your core braced and spine neutral throughout the movement.
Begin the descent by bending at the elbows and lowering your torso in a controlled manner, allowing your elbows to track slightly outward (about 30–45° from the torso) to place emphasis on the chest.
Lower until your shoulders are at or just below elbow level or until you feel a full but comfortable stretch in the chest; avoid excessive depth that causes shoulder pain.
Push back up by driving through the palms and extending the elbows while maintaining the forward lean and chest-up posture; exhale as you press upward.
Return to the start with arms nearly straight but avoid aggressively locking the elbows at the top; maintain control and avoid swinging the body.
Perform repetitions with a steady tempo (e.g., 2–3 seconds down, 1 second pause, 1 second up) and focus on chest contraction each rep.

Progression and safety: if bodyweight is too difficult use assisted dip machine or band assistance; if you want more chest emphasis increase forward lean slightly. Stop if you experience sharp shoulder pain.
exercises.description: A bodyweight dip performed on parallel bars with a forward lean to emphasize the chest; the movement lowers the torso by bending the elbows then pushes the body back up to full arm extension. It targets the pectorals while the triceps and anterior delts assist and stabilize.

exercises.name: Barbell Pullover To Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and hips down (bench‑supported supine position); hold a barbell with a double overhand grip roughly shoulder‑width apart.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and hips down (bench‑supported supine position); hold a barbell with a double overhand grip roughly shoulder‑width apart.
Start with the barbell held vertically over your chest with elbows slightly bent and core braced to maintain a neutral spine.

Inhale and slowly lower the barbell in an arc behind your head by flexing at the shoulders, keeping the upper arms angled and elbows soft until you feel a stretch across the lats/pectorals (do not let the lower back over-arch).
Pause briefly at the bottom of the pullover stretch while maintaining core tension and controlled breathing.
Drive the barbell back in a controlled arc over the chest by contracting the lats and pectorals; as the bar reaches chest level, transition into the press.
Exhale and press the barbell straight up until arms are fully extended overhead, using shoulders and triceps to finish the movement while keeping the ribs down and abs engaged.
Slowly lower the barbell back down to the chest under control to reset for the next rep, maintaining a steady rhythm and avoiding excessive momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Use a manageable weight to protect the shoulders and maintain proper form throughout.
exercises.description: A supine combination movement that begins as a barbell pullover (bringing the weight in an arc behind the head to stretch and work the lats) and finishes as an overhead press, adding shoulder and triceps work. It trains lat length and upper‑body pressing strength while requiring core stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and push your butt back until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend), keeping the spine neutral and eyes looking a few feet ahead.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or pronated grip.
Hinge at the hips and push your butt back until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend), keeping the spine neutral and eyes looking a few feet ahead.
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with weights beneath you; brace your core to prevent rounding of the lower back.
Pull the dumbbells toward your lower ribcage or waist by driving your elbows up and back, squeezing the shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a tight upper back, then lower the weights slowly under control until the arms are extended.
Repeat for the desired reps while keeping the torso still—avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation.
Breathe out as you row the weights up and inhale as you lower them; stop the set if you cannot maintain a neutral spine.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row is a standing, hip-hinged pulling exercise that targets the muscles of the upper back while also engaging the lats and arms. It develops horizontal pulling strength and scapular retraction while the spine and forearms stabilize the position.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm High Row
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a high cable pulley.
instructions: Set a single handle on a high cable pulley. Adjust a bench or chest pad so your chest is firmly supported and you can sit upright without leaning forward or rotating.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest pressed into the support, and grab the handle with a neutral or overhand grip using one arm; start with the arm extended and shoulder slightly protracted.
Brace your core, keep your torso stationary against the chest support, and retract your shoulder blade slightly before initiating the pull.
Pull the handle down and back in a smooth arc by driving your elbow up and back toward your ribcage, focusing on squeezing the upper back and rear delt at the end range.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction while maintaining a neutral wrist and no torso rotation or shrugging, then slowly extend the arm back to the start position under control.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides. Maintain steady breathing (exhale during the pull, inhale during the return) and avoid using momentum.
exercises.description: A single-arm high cable row performed with chest support to isolate the upper back while minimizing torso movement. The movement pulls a high-set handle down and back with the elbow driving toward the ribcage to emphasize the upper back and rear delts.

exercises.name: Cable Alternative Fly
exercises.notes: Set two cable pulleys at a low or mid position (roughly at or slightly below chest height when standing) and attach single-handle grips.
instructions: Set two cable pulleys at a low or mid position (roughly at or slightly below chest height when standing) and attach single-handle grips.
Place a flat bench centered between the cable towers and lie supine (on your back) with feet flat on the floor and head supported by the bench.
Reach up and grab one handle in each hand with a neutral or palms-facing grip; start with the arms out to the sides and elbows slightly bent (soft elbows).
Retract and stabilize your shoulder blades on the bench to minimize shoulder elevation and protect the spine.
Exhale and bring one hand (or alternate hands) in a smooth arc toward the center of your chest, maintaining the same slight elbow bend and keeping the motion driven by the pectorals.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction with the hands meeting or slightly overlapping at midline, squeezing the chest.
Inhale and slowly return the working arm to the start position under control, resisting the cable's pull; keep torso and hips stable and avoid excessive arching of the lower back.
Repeat for the desired reps, alternating arms if performing the alternating variation; keep tempo controlled and avoid using momentum.
exercises.description: A supine cable fly performed on a flat bench where each arm works alternately to bring the handle across the chest, emphasizing horizontal adduction of the shoulder to target the pectorals. The exercise uses a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to isolate the chest.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Incline Bench Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
Load a barbell and grasp it with a shoulder-width reverse (underhand) grip so palms face up and arms hang straight down toward the floor.
Start with your chest pressed into the bench, shoulders relaxed and scapulae slightly protracted, core engaged and neck neutral.

Initiate the movement by retracting your scapulae and pulling the barbell upward in a controlled path toward your lower chest / upper abdomen, leading with the elbows and keeping them close to your sides.
Pause 1 second at the top and squeeze the lats and upper back without hyperextending the spine or lifting your chest off the bench.
Lower the barbell slowly back to the start with full arm extension while maintaining tension in the lats; avoid dropping the weight or using momentum.
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out while pulling and inhale while lowering.
exercises.description: Prone incline bench row performed with a reverse (underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while also engaging the upper back and arms. Lying chest-down on an incline bench, you pull a barbell up toward the lower chest/upper abdomen and lower it under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is leaning forward (torso roughly parallel to the floor or about 30–45° above parallel), knees slightly bent; this is a bent-over position — brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.

Hinge at the hips until your torso is leaning forward (torso roughly parallel to the floor or about 30–45° above parallel), knees slightly bent; this is a bent-over position — brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
Let the dumbbells hang straight down from your shoulders with your arms fully extended; keep your head aligned with your spine.
Initiate the pull by driving your elbows back and up, pulling the dumbbells toward your lower ribs or hip area while squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the top of the movement, maintaining tightness through the lats and upper back without shrugging the shoulders excessively.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled motion until your arms are extended.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, keeping the torso fixed (avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation) and continue to breathe: exhale while pulling, inhale while lowering.
exercises.description: A bent-over row performed with dumbbells where you hinge at the hips and pull the weights toward your torso, emphasizing the lats and upper back. The movement also engages the arms and posterior chain to stabilize the hinged position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent Over Reverse Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is leaned forward (torso roughly parallel or at a 30–45° angle depending on flexibility), keeping the spine neutral and neck aligned.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a reverse (palms-up/supinated) grip.

Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is leaned forward (torso roughly parallel or at a 30–45° angle depending on flexibility), keeping the spine neutral and neck aligned.
Let the arms hang straight toward the floor with the dumbbells under your shoulders; brace your abs to stabilize the spine.
Initiate the row by pulling the dumbbells up and back toward your lower ribs/waist, driving the elbows close to your body and squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the top with scapular retraction, keeping the torso still and avoiding excessive back extension or shrugging.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control until the arms are nearly extended, maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out as you pull and inhaling as you lower.
exercises.description: A bent-over reverse-grip dumbbell row performed from a hip-hinged position to emphasize the upper back and rear shoulder muscles while using a supinated (reverse) grip. The movement pulls the weights toward the torso, engaging the scapular retractors and arm flexors.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent Over Scapula Row
exercises.notes: Set up with one knee and the same-side hand resting on a flat bench (kneeling on bench), the opposite foot planted on the floor for stability; grip a dumbbell in the free hand with the arm hanging straight toward the floor.
instructions: Set up with one knee and the same-side hand resting on a flat bench (kneeling on bench), the opposite foot planted on the floor for stability; grip a dumbbell in the free hand with the arm hanging straight toward the floor.
Hinge forward at the hips so your torso is roughly parallel to the floor while keeping a neutral spine and bracing your core; maintain a slight bend in the supporting knee.

Begin the movement by retracting and depressing the scapula of the working side (pull the shoulder blade back and down) while keeping the elbow nearly straight or allowing only a small bend; do not initiate the movement by bending the elbow.
Squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1 second, feeling the contraction across the upper back and between the shoulder blades.
Slowly allow the scapula to protract and return the arm to the fully extended starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides so the previously supporting hand/knee becomes the working side. Maintain neutral spine and avoid torso rotation throughout.
exercises.description: A bent-over, bench-supported dumbbell row that emphasizes scapular retraction to target the upper back and mid-trapezius/rhomboid area. Performed from a kneeling/bent-over position with a single-arm dumbbell, focusing on pulling the shoulder blade back and down.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
instructions: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
Step 2: Brace your core and gently retract and depress your shoulder blades to stabilize the shoulder girdle throughout the movement.

Step 3: Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, keeping the same slight bend in your elbows and stopping when your upper arms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the chest (avoid dropping below bench level).

Step 4: Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulders, then exhale and drive the dumbbells back up in the same arc by contracting the pectorals until the weights meet or are close together over your sternum.
Step 5: Maintain controlled tempo (no bouncing), keep elbows fixed at the same angle, and avoid locking the arms at the top; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Lying flat on a bench while holding dumbbells, the dumbbell fly opens the arms in a wide arc and brings them together to isolate and stretch the chest (pectorals). Movement emphasizes a controlled, arcing motion with a slight bend in the elbows to protect the shoulder joint.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Straight Arm Pullover
exercises.notes: Brace your core and keep your hips stable (avoid excessive lower-back arch); inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell in a controlled arc behind your head until you feel a deep stretch in the lats/upper chest or your range limit.
instructions: Position yourself supine on a flat bench with your upper back and shoulders supported, feet flat on the floor and knees bent; hold one dumbbell with both hands (palms under one end) above your chest with arms nearly straight and a slight bend in the elbows.

Brace your core and keep your hips stable (avoid excessive lower-back arch); inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell in a controlled arc behind your head until you feel a deep stretch in the lats/upper chest or your range limit.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining the slight elbow bend and scapular stability (do not shrug or let the shoulders round forward).
Exhale and pull the dumbbell back in the same arc to the start position above your chest by driving the motion with the lats and ribcage expansion, keeping the arms mostly straight.
Repeat for the desired reps with slow, controlled tempo; avoid using momentum, excessive elbow bend, or letting the lower back hyperextend.
exercises.description: A supine dumbbell pullover performed with mostly straight arms that moves the weight in an arc from over the chest to behind the head, emphasizing a long-lever stretch and concentric pull to work the lats and upper chest.

exercises.name: Wide Hand Push-up
exercises.notes: Start prone on the floor and place your hands wider than shoulder-width (about 1.
instructions: Start prone on the floor and place your hands wider than shoulder-width (about 1.5x shoulder width) with fingers splayed for stability; extend your legs and balance on your toes so your body forms a straight line from head to heels (high-plank position).
Brace your core and glutes to keep hips level and maintain a neutral neck (look slightly forward or down).
Inhale, then begin lowering by bending the elbows outward (elbows will flare more than in a close-grip push-up) while keeping the shoulders down and away from the ears.
Lower until your chest is close to the floor or your elbows reach about 90 degrees, keeping the body rigid and avoiding hip sag or excessive lumbar arch.
Pause briefly at the bottom with tension in the pectorals and serratus anterior, then exhale and press through the palms to straighten the arms and return to the starting high-plank position.
Keep controlled tempo, maintain full-body tension, and repeat for the desired repetitions. To reduce difficulty, perform the same movement from the knees with hands placed wide.
exercises.description: A prone bodyweight push-up variation with the hands placed wider than shoulder width to increase chest (pectorals) activation. From a high-plank position you lower the torso toward the floor and push back up while maintaining a straight body line.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Palm Rotational Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Keep a neutral spine and neck (eyes toward the floor a few feet in front of you).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand. Slightly bend the knees and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (bent-over position).
Keep a neutral spine and neck (eyes toward the floor a few feet in front of you). Let the dumbbells hang at arm's length beneath your shoulders with a pronated or neutral grip.
Brace your core and initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades back and down (scapular retraction).

Pull the dumbbells upward toward your lower ribs/waist by driving the elbows up and back. As you ascend, rotate your wrists so the palms turn (for example from pronation/neutral toward supination) at the top of the row.
Squeeze the upper back and rear delts at the top for a brief pause, keeping elbows close to the body and avoiding excessive torso rotation or momentum.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position while rotating the palms back to the initial orientation, maintaining control throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps. Maintain the hip hinge, slight knee bend, and neutral spine for the entire set.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row variation where the wrists/palms rotate during the pull to increase scapular retraction and arm involvement. Performed in a hip-hinged position, it emphasizes the upper back while also engaging the lats, rear delts and arms.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width using an overhand or mixed grip.
Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to set the upper back before the first rep.
Pull the barbell upward toward the lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and keeping them close to the body; focus on squeezing the upper back at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a flat back, then lower the barbell under control until arms are nearly extended while preserving the hip-hinge position.
Breathe out while pulling the bar up and inhale while lowering it; keep the torso stable and avoid rounding the lower back or using excessive torso momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, and reset your hip-hinge position between sets if you lose posture.
exercises.description: A hip-hinged barbell row that trains the upper back by pulling the loaded barbell toward the torso while maintaining a neutral spine. It also recruits the lats, arms and posterior chain for stability and power.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Grip Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, maintain a neutral spine, and keep a slight bend in the knees (standing, bent-over position).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a reverse (palms-up / supinated) grip.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, maintain a neutral spine, and keep a slight bend in the knees (standing, bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with the dumbbells under your chest; brace your core and keep the neck in line with the spine.
Initiate the pull by driving the elbows upward and backward, pulling the dumbbells toward the lower ribcage/upper waist while keeping elbows close to the body.
At the top of the movement, pause briefly and squeeze the shoulder blades together without rotating the torso or shrugging the shoulders.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control until the arms are extended, maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine throughout.
Breathe out as you pull the weights up and inhale as you lower them; avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row performed with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip that emphasizes the lats while increasing biceps involvement. The lifter hip-hinges and pulls the dumbbells toward the torso, squeezing the shoulder blades at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and push your butt back until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend), keeping the spine neutral and eyes looking a few feet ahead.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or pronated grip.
Hinge at the hips and push your butt back until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend), keeping the spine neutral and eyes looking a few feet ahead.
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with weights beneath you; brace your core to prevent rounding of the lower back.
Pull the dumbbells toward your lower ribcage or waist by driving your elbows up and back, squeezing the shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a tight upper back, then lower the weights slowly under control until the arms are extended.
Repeat for the desired reps while keeping the torso still—avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation.
Breathe out as you row the weights up and inhale as you lower them; stop the set if you cannot maintain a neutral spine.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row is a standing, hip-hinged pulling exercise that targets the muscles of the upper back while also engaging the lats and arms. It develops horizontal pulling strength and scapular retraction while the spine and forearms stabilize the position.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Wide Grip Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor; keep your spine neutral and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and place a loaded barbell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor; keep your spine neutral and head in line with the spine.
Grip the bar with a wide overhand grip (wider than shoulder-width) and let your arms hang fully extended.
Brace your core, inhale, then pull the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows out and back, squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders retracted, then exhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner until arms are extended.
Repeat for the desired repetitions while avoiding torso rotation, excessive momentum, or spinal rounding; keep knees slightly bent and maintain the hip hinge throughout.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row performed with a wide overhand grip that emphasizes the upper back by retracting the shoulder blades and pulling the bar toward the lower chest/upper abdomen. The torso stays hinged forward while the hips and spine stabilize the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
instructions: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
Step 2: Brace your core and gently retract and depress your shoulder blades to stabilize the shoulder girdle throughout the movement.

Step 3: Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, keeping the same slight bend in your elbows and stopping when your upper arms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the chest (avoid dropping below bench level).

Step 4: Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulders, then exhale and drive the dumbbells back up in the same arc by contracting the pectorals until the weights meet or are close together over your sternum.
Step 5: Maintain controlled tempo (no bouncing), keep elbows fixed at the same angle, and avoid locking the arms at the top; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Lying flat on a bench while holding dumbbells, the dumbbell fly opens the arms in a wide arc and brings them together to isolate and stretch the chest (pectorals). Movement emphasizes a controlled, arcing motion with a slight bend in the elbows to protect the shoulder joint.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm High Row
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a high cable pulley.
instructions: Set a single handle on a high cable pulley. Adjust a bench or chest pad so your chest is firmly supported and you can sit upright without leaning forward or rotating.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest pressed into the support, and grab the handle with a neutral or overhand grip using one arm; start with the arm extended and shoulder slightly protracted.
Brace your core, keep your torso stationary against the chest support, and retract your shoulder blade slightly before initiating the pull.
Pull the handle down and back in a smooth arc by driving your elbow up and back toward your ribcage, focusing on squeezing the upper back and rear delt at the end range.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction while maintaining a neutral wrist and no torso rotation or shrugging, then slowly extend the arm back to the start position under control.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides. Maintain steady breathing (exhale during the pull, inhale during the return) and avoid using momentum.
exercises.description: A single-arm high cable row performed with chest support to isolate the upper back while minimizing torso movement. The movement pulls a high-set handle down and back with the elbow driving toward the ribcage to emphasize the upper back and rear delts.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squeeze Bench Press
exercises.notes: Step 1: Sit at the end of a flat bench and bring two dumbbells together so their heads touch; lie back so you are supine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent.
instructions: Step 1: Sit at the end of a flat bench and bring two dumbbells together so their heads touch; lie back so you are supine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent.
Step 2: Hold the dumbbells together at mid-chest with palms facing each other (neutral grip) and actively squeeze the dumbbells together to create tension in the chest and serratus anterior.
Step 3: Brace your core and keep a slight natural arch in the lower back (do not over-arch); keep the dumbbells touching and elbows tucked to about a 30–45° angle from the torso.
Step 4: Exhale and press the dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until the arms are nearly extended while maintaining the inward squeeze; avoid fully locking the elbows.

Step 5: Pause briefly at the top while continuing to squeeze the dumbbells together, then inhale and slowly lower them back to the starting position at the mid-chest, maintaining contact between the dumbbells.
Step 6: Repeat for the desired number of reps, focusing on constant squeeze and controlled tempo; stop or reduce weight if form breaks or shoulders begin to compensate.
exercises.description: A supine dumbbell press variation where two dumbbells are held together and squeezed throughout the movement to increase chest activation and inner-pec engagement. Press the paired dumbbells up from the chest while maintaining constant inward pressure.

exercises.name: Cable Close Grip Front Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Attach a close-grip/V-bar or place hands close together on a narrow bar; grasp the handle with a firm, neutral/pronated grip (hands close together).
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown bench and secure your thighs under the pads so your torso cannot rise during the movement (seated position).
Attach a close-grip/V-bar or place hands close together on a narrow bar; grasp the handle with a firm, neutral/pronated grip (hands close together).
Start with arms extended overhead, chest up and a slight lean backward from the hips (about 10–20°) to put tension on the lats (slight recline).
Initiate the pull by driving your elbows down and back, leading with the elbows rather than the hands, and pull the bar to the upper chest/upper sternum.
At the bottom of the movement, squeeze your lats and upper back for a brief 1–2 second pause while keeping the torso stable and not using momentum.
Slowly extend your arms back to the start in a controlled manner until your lats are under tension again (full but not locked extension).
Repeat for the desired number of reps; keep core engaged and avoid excessive swinging or pulling with the lower back.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a close grip to emphasize the latissimus dorsi while minimizing shoulder abduction; a narrow grip increases biceps and upper-back involvement. Performed seated with thigh pads to stabilize the body while pulling the handle to the upper chest.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown (straight arm)
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar, rope, or single handle to a high pulley.
instructions: Attach a straight bar, rope, or single handle to a high pulley. Select a moderate weight that allows controlled movement while keeping arms mostly straight.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart (or a slight stagger for balance). Grasp the handle with both hands (pronated or neutral grip) and step back slightly so the cable is taut.

Hinge slightly forward at the hips so your torso leans about 10–20 degrees; keep knees soft and maintain a neutral spine. Arms should be extended in front of you with a small, fixed bend in the elbows (not fully locked).

Initiate the rep by driving the shoulders into extension: press the hands downward and back in a controlled arc until the hands reach thigh level and you feel maximal contraction in the lats. Keep the elbows stationary and avoid bending them to pull.
Pause briefly at the bottom while squeezing the lats and maintaining straight-arm tension.
Slowly return the handle to the starting position under control, keeping constant tension on the cable and avoiding letting the shoulders fully relax at the top.
Breathe out during the downward (working) phase and inhale while returning to the start. Perform desired number of reps with steady tempo.
exercises.description: Standing straight-arm cable pushdowns target the lats by driving the arms down from a high cable with minimal elbow flexion. The movement emphasizes shoulder extension and scapular retraction while keeping the torso stable.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at the hips and lean forward slightly so your torso is at about a 30–45° angle (standing, hip-hinged position).
Grab the bar with an overhand grip hands shoulder-width apart and extend your arms fully in front of you so the cable is taut; maintain a small, natural bend in the elbows (do not hyperextend).
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to engage the lats before initiating the movement.

Keeping the arms straight (maintaining the slight elbow bend), drive the bar down in an arc toward the thighs by extending the shoulder (pulling the upper arms down and back) until the bar reaches hip or upper-thigh level.
Pause briefly at the bottom and squeeze the lats and upper back while maintaining a stable, braced core and neutral spine (avoid rounding or excessive lumbar extension).
Slowly return the bar to the start position with control, allowing the shoulders to flex while maintaining tension in the lats; repeat for desired reps.

Common form tips: keep the torso still (avoid using momentum or excessive torso lean), maintain a slight elbow bend, and focus on scapular depression and lat contraction rather than pulling with the forearms.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-arm pulldowns hinge the torso forward and pull a high-set cable down with mostly straight arms to target the latissimus dorsi. The movement emphasizes scapular depression and shoulder extension while the core stabilizes the body.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternate Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows bent.
instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows bent.
Brace your abs and keep your chest up to maintain a neutral spine; avoid overarching the lower back.
Press one dumbbell overhead by extending the elbow and driving through the shoulder until the arm is nearly or fully straight (do not violently lock the elbow).
Pause briefly at the top, keeping the shoulder engaged and scapula stable (avoid shrugging excessively).
Lower that dumbbell under control back to shoulder height while maintaining core tension.
Immediately press the opposite dumbbell overhead, alternating arms with each repetition in a steady, controlled rhythm.
Breathe out during each press and inhale as you lower; choose a weight that allows full control and good form for the desired rep range.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell shoulder press where you press one dumbbell overhead at a time, alternating arms to build shoulder strength and unilateral stability. Maintain a braced core and controlled tempo while lowering and pressing each weight.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set the pulleys to the lowest position and attach single D-handles; select an appropriate weight on both sides.
instructions: Set the pulleys to the lowest position and attach single D-handles; select an appropriate weight on both sides.

Stand centered between the two stations with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grasp a handle in each hand with a neutral (thumb-up) grip, arms hanging down at your sides.
Cross the cables in front of you if the setup requires it so there is slight tension at the start; keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the exercise.
Brace your core and keep your torso upright (no leaning or swinging). Initiate the movement by lifting the arms out to the sides (lead with the elbows) until your hands reach about shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders down and back (do not shrug), then slowly lower the handles back to the start under control.
Repeat for the desired reps, exhaling as you lift and inhaling as you lower; avoid using momentum or excessive torso tilt to complete reps.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys that isolates the lateral deltoid by abducting the arms out to the sides to shoulder height while keeping the torso stable. The cable setup provides constant tension through the movement for controlled concentric and eccentric phases.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows slightly in front of the body.
Keep the spine neutral and ribcage down to avoid hyperextending the lower back; squeeze the abs throughout.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead without locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders stacked over hips and the head neutral (you may slightly move your head back to allow the dumbbells to pass), keeping shoulder blades stable.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding excessive torso lean or lower-back arching; if needed, use a lighter weight to maintain good form.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press is a compound shoulder exercise where you press dumbbells from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, primarily working the delts while engaging the triceps and upper back for support and stabilization.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Supinated Face Pull (with rope)
exercises.notes: Set a cable pulley to about eye to upper-chest height and attach a rope handle.
instructions: Set a cable pulley to about eye to upper-chest height and attach a rope handle.
Stand facing the machine in a staggered or hip-width stance with a slight bend in the knees and a neutral spine.
Reach forward and grab the rope ends with a supinated (palms-up) grip so your thumbs point toward you; step back until there is tension in the cable and your arms are nearly extended.
Brace your core, exhale, and pull the rope toward your face by driving your elbows out to the sides and externally rotating the shoulders so the hands move toward the ears.
At the end of the movement squeeze the shoulder blades together and pause 1–2 seconds while keeping the torso stable (avoid leaning back or shrugging).
Inhale and slowly extend the arms forward to the start position under control, maintaining tension in the cable.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping tempo controlled and focusing on rear delt and upper-back contraction rather than using momentum.
exercises.description: Standing cable face pull with a supinated (palms-up) rope grip emphasizes the rear delts while bringing the shoulder blades together and externally rotating the shoulders. Pull the rope toward your face with elbows high, then return under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Bent Arm Lateral raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Bend your elbows to about 90 degrees so the dumbbells are in front of your thighs and palms face down or slightly toward each other.
Keeping the elbows bent and the torso still, raise your forearms and elbows out to the sides until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor (elbows stay higher than the hands).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining shoulder blade stability; avoid excessive shrugging or leaning the torso.
Slowly lower the weights back to the start under control, keeping tension on the delts and avoiding swinging.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining neutral spine and steady breathing throughout.
exercises.description: A standing lateral raise variation performed with the elbows bent to shorten the lever arm and focus tension on the lateral delts. Lift the dumbbells out to the sides until the upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor, then lower with control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
instructions: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, keep a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (bent-over position).
Let your arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a slight bend in the elbows; maintain a braced core and avoid rounding the back.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the ground (level with the shoulders).
Squeeze the rear delts and shoulder blades together at the top for a brief pause, avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control, resisting any momentum; repeat for desired reps.
Keep the movement strict: use lighter weight if you must swing the torso or bend the elbows excessively, and breathe out on the lift and in on the lower.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell raise that isolates the rear (posterior) deltoids by lifting the arms out to the sides while the torso is hinged forward. It emphasizes shoulder horizontal abduction and scapular retraction with a controlled tempo.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

workout.title: Shoulders/Arms - BODYBUILD/ENDURANCE
workout.description: Shoulders/Arms workout focusing on bodybuild and endurance training.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Alternate Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level (front-rack position) with palms facing forward or slightly inward.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level (front-rack position) with palms facing forward or slightly inward.
Keep the wrist neutral and elbow beneath the dumbbell, scapulae stable and chest up; inhale to prepare and engage the abs.
Exhale and press one dumbbell straight overhead by extending the elbow and driving through the shoulder until the arm is fully extended overhead without arching the lower back.
Pause briefly at the top with the arm locked out and the dumbbell balanced over the shoulder/hip line, then control the descent back to the shoulder (front-rack) position.
As you lower the pressed arm, immediately repeat the press with the opposite arm so you alternate sides each rep; keep the non-pressing arm stable at rack height to help balance.
Continue alternating reps while maintaining a neutral spine, tight abs, and minimal torso rotation; avoid using leg drive or leaning back to lift heavier weight.
Perform repetitions in a controlled tempo and choose a weight that allows full range of motion while keeping proper form.
exercises.description: A standing single-arm (alternating) dumbbell press that develops shoulder strength and stability by pressing one weight overhead at a time while the other arm remains at rack position. Maintain a tight core and neutral spine to control the movement and prevent torso lean.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Cuban Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body).
Begin the movement by driving your elbows up and out in a controlled upright-row motion, keeping the dumbbells close to the body until your elbows reach roughly shoulder height.

With elbows elevated, externally rotate at the shoulders so your forearms move forward and the dumbbells are brought in front of your face/chest area (upper arms should be roughly parallel to the floor).
From that externally rotated position, press the dumbbells straight overhead until your arms are fully extended, keeping the core tight and avoiding excessive back arch.

Lower the dumbbells back down to the externally rotated position in a controlled manner, then rotate the forearms back to the upright-row position and lower the weights to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, using a weight that allows smooth, controlled rotation and full overhead lockout without shrugging the shoulders.
exercises.description: A standing compound shoulder movement that combines an upright row, external rotation, and overhead press to build deltoid strength and shoulder stability while recruiting the traps and triceps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench so the back pad is at a low angle and stand facing the side of the bench.
instructions: Set an incline bench so the back pad is at a low angle and stand facing the side of the bench.
Pick up a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip and take a hip-width stance with a slight bend in the knees.
Hinge forward at the hips and place your forehead or chest gently against the bench pad for support so your torso is close to horizontal (bench-assisted bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a small bend in the elbows; keep a neutral spine and the head supported on the bench.
Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc — lead with the elbows — until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and you feel the rear delts contract.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting position.
Keep the movement strict (no torso rotation or momentum) and maintain head/chest support on the bench throughout the set.
Choose a weight that allows controlled form for the full range of motion and perform recommended reps.
exercises.description: A bent-over, bench-supported dumbbell raise that targets the rear (posterior) delts by moving the arms out to the sides while the torso is stabilized. The head/chest is supported on an incline bench to reduce momentum and isolate the rear deltoids.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent Over Reverse Raise (skier)
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and a dumbbell in each hand held at arm's length.
instructions: Start standing with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and a dumbbell in each hand held at arm's length.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (bent-over position), keeping a neutral spine and neck; let the dumbbells hang beneath your shoulders with palms facing each other.
Brace your core and keep a slight bend in the elbows throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor, leading with the elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a neutral spine, then inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position under control.
Avoid using momentum or rotating the torso — keep the hips steady and the movement isolated to the shoulder girdle.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining consistent form and controlled tempo.
exercises.description: Standing bent at the hips with a neutral spine, this reverse raise (skier) moves dumbbells outward in a horizontal arc to emphasize the posterior delts and upper upper-back musculature. It strengthens the rear shoulder while promoting scapular retraction and postural control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Front Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing the body) and arms resting by your sides.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing the body) and arms resting by your sides.
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and maintain a neutral spine and tucked chin throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise both dumbbells straight forward from the hips to about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor), leading with the elbows and keeping the wrists neutral.
Pause briefly at the top while keeping shoulders down (avoid shrugging) and avoid using body swing or back extension to lift the weight.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, choosing a weight that allows strict form; if form breaks, reduce the weight or perform single-arm raises to maintain control.
exercises.description: A standing resistance exercise where you raise dumbbells straight in front of you to shoulder height to emphasize the anterior delts. It uses a controlled motion with core bracing to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Supinated Face Pull (with rope)
exercises.notes: Set a cable pulley to about eye to upper-chest height and attach a rope handle.
instructions: Set a cable pulley to about eye to upper-chest height and attach a rope handle.
Stand facing the machine in a staggered or hip-width stance with a slight bend in the knees and a neutral spine.
Reach forward and grab the rope ends with a supinated (palms-up) grip so your thumbs point toward you; step back until there is tension in the cable and your arms are nearly extended.
Brace your core, exhale, and pull the rope toward your face by driving your elbows out to the sides and externally rotating the shoulders so the hands move toward the ears.
At the end of the movement squeeze the shoulder blades together and pause 1–2 seconds while keeping the torso stable (avoid leaning back or shrugging).
Inhale and slowly extend the arms forward to the start position under control, maintaining tension in the cable.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping tempo controlled and focusing on rear delt and upper-back contraction rather than using momentum.
exercises.description: Standing cable face pull with a supinated (palms-up) rope grip emphasizes the rear delts while bringing the shoulder blades together and externally rotating the shoulders. Pull the rope toward your face with elbows high, then return under control.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
instructions: Stand upright with feet together or hip-width apart, step on the middle of the band to anchor it securely under both feet.
Hold a handle in each hand with palms facing your body and arms hanging at your sides; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your abs and keep your torso tall and still; avoid leaning or using momentum.
Exhale and lift both arms out to the sides leading with the elbows until your hands reach shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Keep wrists neutral and maintain the slight elbow bend throughout the movement; do not shrug the shoulders upward.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the delts, then inhale and slowly lower the arms back to the starting position under control.
Repeat for desired reps, maintaining smooth, controlled movement and stable posture on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing lateral raise using a resistance band where you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height to emphasize the lateral delts. The band provides continuous tension while the traps and forearms assist for stability and grip.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.
